At the end of the rolls, movement is figured as such.
-If all four are stampedes the player will roll all 4 die one time and move BACKWARDS the number of spaces totaled up on the roll.
-For the total of all Ones, Twos, and Threes the player will move that many spaces FORWARD minus one space for each grazing die. 
-For each Runaway that has not been lassoed, the player moves BACKWARDS one space.
-For each Runaway that has been lassoed the player will move forward TWICE the total number of spaces listed.

The Ungame - Catholic Version is a redesign of the classic Ungame packaged with the updated standard Ungame board and two new decks of cards with questions specifically meant to start discussion on the Catholic faith of the game participants.

Players roll the dice and travel around the game board answering questions along the way. The journey's the thing, as there is no finish line and no losers.

Players will answer questions from one of two decks. Deck one is light hearted and meant to break the ice. Deck two is deeper and meant to be more thought provoking.

Examples of questions from the publisher:

If you could meet three famous Catholics from past or present, who would you choose? Why?

Do you have a patron saint? Who? Why did you choose that person?

What do you think HEAVEN is like?

How does your Catholic faith show in your actions?
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
In this fun and frisky board game made by gays for gays, each player takes on the part of one of rich old man Jonathan Glamcock's household employees.  Jonathan only has 9 days left to live and he is willing to leave his entire fortune to the houseboy who proves most dutiful.

Of course, the boys are all young and randy and have to find a way to balance doing their chores to earn points and getting busy with their peers without getting caught on the cameras the old man has installed throughout the mansion.

Gameplay is easy, quick and fun. On your turn, you must move to a different room in the mansion and either do CHORES (rolling dice to place cubes for points) or HOOKUP with another houseboy in order to gain more cubes if you're running low. You keep rolling your dice until your boy rolls a tired icon and ends his chore or a camera icon that ends the hookup.

The player with the most cubes on the board at the end of the game will earn Jonathan Glamcock's entire fortune!

One of the two finalists in the Cadburys Pocketgame competition, Flick Racer was designed by James Wallis. Pocketgames are designed to be portable, quick to play and easy to learn, and Flick Racer does that. A dexterity game along the lines of PitchCar / Carabande, racers must flick their car discs (made from recycled tyres!) around a course drawn using the included stick of chalk.

Egg-a-thon is a free game given out by Cadbury's and Royal Mail to celebrate the 2012 Olympic games.

The aim of the game is to move your two pieces from one side of the board to the other by rolling and moving the rolled number. the bad egg roll is miss a go, and the good egg roll is move one space and roll again.

The game includes two traffic cones but no rules for them. the rules state they are for you to be creative with.

The board is an 11x4 grid so a total of 22 is required to win, hoever you can move to block others if you wish. Overall a very simple game with very limited strategy.

boardgame: Drag Show: The Contentious Drinking Game
description:
In this fast-paced card game each player takes on the role of a drag queen trying to out-perform her peers at the club.  On your turn you can play one card from your hand and the first player to earn 10 points of GLAM wins!

"Show Move" cards, such as "Shimmy the Girls" and "Hit the High Note", benefit your own performance and give you points.  "Bitch Move" cards, such as "Smack Talk" and "Kick Her Canckles", hinder the other players' performances and make them lose points.

You could also play nasty-sounding drink cards, such as the "Turd Spackle", on a player which slowly gets them drunk and makes them lose points every turn until  they can pass it on.

Fabulous card art from Stan Yan and witty catch phrases from Layman Kingsford help to inspire players to get catty with each other in true drag queen style.

The Russo-Georgian War of 2008 is a DTP simulation game covering the military action during that conflict from August 8 2008 through August 12, 2008.  This involved full-on combat between the Republic of Georgia and the Russian Federation plus two breakaway regions of Georgia; Abkhazia and South Ossetia. The fighting began in the Ossetia (the primary Georgian target) and quickly spread to all of Georgia and Abkhazia.

At first glance this seems like a fight between David and Goliath - the vast Russian military against a state with only about 20,000 active duty personnel in it's military and only 7 combat jets - but closer examination reveals it was a somewhat stiffer competition with several points of interest.  It was with this thought that this game was inspired.

The game is relatively straight forward to learn and play and the system, while adapted to the conflict, very familiar to most wargamers.  Learning and playing the game can be done fairly quickly.  The game is play-balanced due to a selection of real-world scenarios, including the historical situation.

This is a Desk Top Published (DTP)game - gluing and cutting of counters is required.

SCALE - Game Turns represent 12 hours, units typically represent battallions of 500 - 1000 men plus some smaller and larger, units and hexes on the map are approximately 12 kilometers across.

Früher oder Später is a card game issued by the Bundeszentrale für politische Bildung of Germany. The game has 61 cards, each one depicting one event of German history from 1949-2010.

The game contains two variants. The first variant is a simple guessing game if the hidden card from the top of your stack is earlier or later than the current open card. The second game is a bit more interesting for gamers. One player reads an event and the other players offer one of their events which should be as close as possible to the first event. The first player then decides which event is closest to his event. If he's right, he and the other player can keep the card. Otherwise, only the player who has played the closest card, can keep his card. Winner is the player who first gets 5 cards.

A game about survival. The players represent a group of survivors of a helicopter crash in the middle of the Amazon rainforest. Each player has eigth starting objects and the goal of the game is finding nearest village (the end of the board). The first player to succeed wins.

Using the dice to advance, every now and then some player lands on a red dot, when this happens a "situation card" is revealed an readed out loud: some situation cards presents the player with opportunities while other with problems, for example you may need some object to advance, or some object to affect other players. Some cards represents goblal situations that affect all the players.

Also there's some trading and bluffing element in the way of how the game manages objects. Note: i got this game as a present for chrismas in 1993 but there's no record of the year of publishing whatsoever.

A fusion of Dominion and Touhou Project.  An unusual event is occuring in Gensokyo, a paradise where humans, youkai, ghosts, aliens and gods dwell in.

As a collaboration with Dominion, the game uses the same mechanic as Dominion.  Each player construct their own deck from a common pool of available cards, and the person with the most victory point wins at the end.

Consists of 485 cards, with illustrations by 28 illustrators.
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
Players collect animals through an unique auction system. First, an active player decide the price of the animal tiles. Then, everyone simultaneously decides either to accept or to refuse this offer. This process can be repeated at most three times. There is a bonus when exactly one player wants the tiles but no one knows who bid what except the active player.

Also players would like to collect all 5 animals but having an excess of animals will cost you at the end of the game.

User summary
In the game of ドナドナ (DONNA DONNA), players try to collect valuable animals through auctions.

In an auction, the active player declare a price for auctioned animals, and the other players secretly decides whether they accept or decline the offer.

At end of the game, you score points according to variety and quantity of animals you have acquired. However, each animal has an acquisition limit, and you will be taxed if you have acquired animals of that type more than the limit. The highest scorer wins the game.

このゲームは、競りゲームです。各プレイヤーは、より高価値となるであろう動物タイルを競りによって獲得することを目指します。
競りでは、ターンプレイヤーが販売希望価格を宣言し、他のプレイヤーがそれに応じるか否かを秘密裡に回答することで進行していきます。
各プレイヤーのポイントは獲得した動物タイルの種類数と枚数によって決まります。ただし、各動物ごとに非課税対象枚数が決まっており、それを超えると税金がかかります。
ゲーム終了時に、最もポイントの高いプレイヤーの勝ちとなります。

Set in March 1945, as the Second World War draws to a close, Zombiekrieg 848 allows players to replay the battles fought  between Allied troops and an enemy that history does not record. 
  It is a simple set of tabletop miniature wargame rules that can be played solo if desired. All players take the side of Allied troops, with the hoard Abteilung 848 zombies being controlled by a reaction table.

Geheimkrieg is a set of dark science WW2 combat rules written by Matthew Hartley, Steve Blease and Michael Baumann .

The game is a set of company level wargames rules written for miniatures from 20mm to 36mm (1/48th) scale. They recreate the ultra-secret war fought between the Western Allies and the Nazis in the last years of World War II. Refight the war you never knew happened...

Includes:
- A completely new set of highly innovative quick-play wargames rules that involve no measuring!
- The recently uncovered history of what really happened in WWII.
- Scenarios set in the top secret war.
- Statistics for historical and secret troop types for Britain, Germany and USA.

Guinness World Records DVD-Ennätyspeli is a trivia game about world records. Players or teams are answering questions and for every right answer a chip is awarded, the first player or team to get 10 chips is the winner. Most of the questions are on the cards, but some of them come from the DVD, most of those have a video about a record attempt and the question is about the video. There are also challenges where two players go head to head in a simple task like listing names or drawing a house - the winner of the challenge recieves two chips.

Trivia game themed on famous places and persons connected to Swedish speaking Finns.

Questions are divided into five categories, and there are Easy and Difficult questions to choose from. You need to answer a question correctly to move forward. Answer an easy question correctly and you move one step forward, answer a difficult question and you move two steps.

First person completing a lap around the board wins.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From Pagat:

Mitch uses a standard 52 card deck, with cards ranked from King high, to Two low.

The goal of the game is to win suits, by having more cards in that suit when the play ends than the other players have.

Suits are collected in columns. Each column contains cards of a single suit, arranged in descending order from top to bottom. You cannot have more than one column of the same suit. You can only add a card of a suit that you already have if it is lower in rank than the lowest card in your column for that suit.

The game ends shortly after the third Ace is drawn.

From the cover (translated): "Thomas is looking for his friends. Can you help him find Percy, Gordon, James and the other trains? Besides a good memory you need to be able to work together in this game to succeed. For only together will you be able to beat Diesel."

Thomas needs to find his friends, and goes looking for them in various different stations, trying to avoid running into the mean Diesel. His friends need to be found in a particular order. If he runs into Diesel once too often (5 times), then the players lose.

This is a cooperative game in which the players work together to try to defeat Diesel. They can help each other with remembering where the particular friend is whom they're looking for.

This game involves memory, mainly, but it's as much a cooperative activity which makes for fun game-play.
family: TV Shows: Thomas the Tank Engine

boardgame: New Moon: The Movie Card Game
description:
New Moon: The Movie Card game seems to be a 'simplified' version of The Board Game.

Players take turns drawing cards, which may result in an activity or trivia question.  These activities may be dice battles, secret balloting, or about your friends.

Collect cards by being right.  First player to collect eight cards is the winner.

Comes in a tin box.

This game is based on a 7 by 8 grid made to look like a football pitch.  Each player has 5 outfiled players and a goalie.  Movement is by rolling a dice and players can challenge for possession of the ball.

Players play the game for a fixed amount of time, decided beforehand, and if the result is a draw, then there is a penalty shootout to decide the winner.

The game is supplied in a metal tin and the pieces are also magnetic, so the game is also portable.

Memory-, Domino- and quizgame in one, about everything Dutch! 'Kaaskop', 'Klompendans', 'Tulp', 'Trekdrop', 'Hinkelen' and 'Haasje-over'. With 50 authentic photo's and 90 'Orange'-quenstions!

Contents:
25 Photocards with 50 photo's
15 Quizcards with 90 questions

This is a nice party game, where players need to remember parts of the typical Dutch pictures, answer typical dutch questions and try to combine the remembered pictures. The player who combines four pictures to make one wins!
Tiles are, facing down, spread out on the table. Every turn a player turns a card face up, showing parts of 4 different pictures. The player has to answer a typical dutch question. Answer correct; you may have the tile. Wrong answer; the tile has to be turned down again. Then it's the next players turn.

When a player collects the 4 pieces to complete a picture he/she is the winner and gets the title of 'Dutch Expert' ;)

Mario Kard

Mario Kard is a free PnP version of the popular Mario Kart videogame saga. So, this is a racing game composed of four kind of cards: 
1. Road Cards destinated to make modular circuits.
2. Object Cards representing special items that drivers can use for themselves or against the others, these are the well known shells, mushrooms, stars,...
3. Character Cards for each driver with his atributes: speed cards, first card, brake, attack and defense.
4. Speed Cards; each driver has a set of four speed cards to determinate the movements and aceleration.

Drivers must move using Speed Cards. If you want play Child Mode you can use a D6. 
D6 is needed for attack and defense.

-Preparing the game

First you have to build a circuit and decide the total amount of laps to win the race. There must be at least one finish line and one special Road Card  where objects appear. Then each player chooses a driver (or randomly) and takes his Speed Cards. In the Driver Card is determined what is the first Speed Card to use (that is the acceleration rate somehow).

-The Race
Player generally moves using a Speed Card that limits the maximun positions he can move on. He'll choose to move more or less, but allways within that limit.

-Start:
Put Drivers on/beside finish line. Then each driver takes a random Speed Card and moves in turn. The first will be the
younger player and then continue clockwise.

On the road:
Each turn, every player must choose secretly one Speed Card; then Speed Cards are shown and drivers move.

Get objects:
In the circiut there will be a Road Card with the object icon. When drivers pass on this, they get a Object Card from the Object Card deck. Drivers only can have one object card.

Attack/defense:
When drivers are moving they could attack other players using Object Cards. The result is decided using Drivers Attack/Defense values, Object Attack/Defense values and a D6. Something like this:

Bowser attacks Mario with a Red Shell:
  Bowser attack + Red Shell attack + D6 > Mario defense + D6

Total attacker value must be greater than total defender value; if equal then nothing happens. Some objects could be used to add more Defense value (shells, bananas,...)

If the attack is successful the loser goes off the road, loses any Object Card and must wait one turn without moving. Then He must start using Drivers first Speed Card.

-Dangerous Curves:
Some Road Cards have a special values like -1,-2,+1. These cards decrease Driver movement unless the Driver has a brake value bigger than 0. 
So if a player must go through a -2 curve and has a +1 brake value the movement decreases is only -1. Anyway if the driver finish a movement in a curve this decrease is not applied.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description: It’s Hello Kitty and friends in a kids’ first bingo game. Oversized dice reveal the letter and a picture. If you can find a match on your card, place a token there. Be the first player to cover 5 squares in a row to win. Includes an extra die and rules for older players. For 2 to 6 players, ages 4 and up.

Goal: to discover the secret word from every other player based on images and verbal clues.

How to play: Every player draws a card and spins a wheel to define wich side of the card must be used. Then each player spins a wheel and asks the player on his left something based on the result of the wheel:
- 1 vowel
- 2 consonants
- First letter
- Letter before
- How many letter?
- Last letter
- Clue
- How many consonants
- How many vowels

The first player to guess everyone´s word wins the round and get the cards of all other players. After 5 rounds, the player with the most cards wins the game.

This is an economic simulation game where each player is the manager of a multinational company. These companies produce and sell the same good (but in different styles and qualities) in 4 different regions (North/South America, Europe, Asia). Each game year the players will need to make important investment decisions (such as constructing or shutting down shops/factories worldwide, buying/selling shares, paying dividends), select a viable and efficient production plan, try to fulfill it and finally convert their production to sales. Also, they will have to look at the competition and through research try to push their efficiency even further. Simultaneously, local providers will also offer their products and "steal" a share of the market.

As in reality selling does not come easily. Each region will have a specific demand for the homogenous good (but in different quantities and styles) and a specific preference for quality and style. Considering these factors and of course the price, players will try to manipulate the markets. Demand is not totally known, however there is always a way to know better and possibly "change" the knowledge of the competitors at the last moment. Difficult decisions and a bit of luck will define the actual sales.

Players try to make profits not only for the money but also for their share price. Otherwise they will have to pay dividends if they want to keep the share price from declining. Loans are limited, costly and should not be taken lightly.

Scoring is unlocked by profitability and is measured by share price. Thankfully, no ownership changes are taking place in this game (it is assumed that the initial number of shares represent less than 50% of the company).

Dragon's Lord is a free non-CCG about duels between dragon trainers. With one shared deck the two players will be able to make their dragons grow and fight in the quest for the title "Dragon's Lord". Every turn you can put in play from your hand one dragon, attach one energy card to a dragon, play one tactic card, and/or attack; then the next player draws one card and repeats the process. Once you have in play a basic dragon you can put in play a level one dragon, then a level 2, and so on; or you can level up the basic dragon and get a bonus ability. Game play and style is similar to Pokémon TCG but much simpler, yet very strategic.

Dragon's Lord es un juego gratuito de cartas no coleccionable sobre duelos entre entrenadores de dragones. Con un único mazo compartido los dos jugadores serán capaces de hacer crecer sus dragones y luchar en busca del título de "Señor de los Dragones". Cada turno puedes poner en juego desde tu mano un dragón, adosar una carta de energía a un dragón, jugar una carta de táctica y/o atacar; luego el siguiente jugador roba una carta y repite el proceso. Una vez que tienes en juego un dragón básico puedes poner en juego un dragón de nvl 1, luego uno de nvl 2 y así sucesivamente. O puedes hacer subir de nivel el dragón básico y obtener una habilidad especial adicional. El juego — mecánica y estilo — es similar a Pokémon TCG pero mucho más simple y a la vez estratégico.

No Problem! The Number Strategy Game, a dice game for ages 10 to Adult.

Contents: 11 dice (6 white d6, 5 green with math symbols); 1-minute hourglass timer, dice cup, and score sheets.

Objective: To throw the dice and get the highest score possible by arranging the dice in the time allowed, alternating numerical die with math symbol die.

Lots of rules variations provided: Educational version (use normal math rules and order of operations); Family version (calculate from left to right ignoring normal math rules); play with or without the timer; play with fewer dice; play for lowest total rather than highest. Play by yourself as a challenge, or with any number of people. Can be played in teams. Total scores for each player or team for up to 5 rounds for Grand Total.

Top Familiespel is a game where players try to obtain all products listed on a shopping list. They do so by rolling a die to move on the board, trying to get to the items they need. The first person to get all the items and return to the cash register is the winner.

The pawns are in the shape of shopping carts, made from plastic.
The items come in the form of thick cardboard pieces which will be randomly placed on the board. Each card has a picture of the product printed on it. (User summary)

Build your business empire to be the first player to earn $1 billion.  Start out with $100k.  Buy buying companies, cutting deals, and sometimes robbing the bank, work your way up into the super rich.

Turn-based game for up to four players.  A roll of the dice determines which country will earn money and where you will have the chance to purchase new businesses.  People cards work to sabotage your opponents, or protect you from sabotage.  An unlucky roll results in a new flash of the penalty, or bonus, for your opponents' choice.

Based on the hit T.V. Gameshow Talkin' 'bout your Generation

Challenge your family, entertain your friends and befuddle your rivals as you test your knowledge of popular culture.

Teams of 2 play as either Gen X, Y or Baby Boomers
With a seprate set of questions to go with each Generation

Teams answer questions given by the Quick Fire cards. Teams can answer questions and can continue to answer questions until the team reaches the end of that round's spaces.

Then play passes to the the next team. You can move forward in the next turn.

The comes the Magic Window round. Each magic window card has a single question on it. It could be for example: "Celebrity Convictions" in which you have to guess the celebrity's conviction. Teams can answer questions and can continue to answer questions until the team reaches the end of that round's spaces.

Then comes the Your Gen round in which each card has three trivia questions from their generation.Teams can answer questions and can continue to answer questions until the team reaches the end of that round's spaces.

When End Game is reached by one team they must stop until the other teams reach the End Game space. On each End Game card a physical challenge is written for example: "Build the tallest house of cards in a minute"

Includes: Game Board, 200 Cards, 6 Tokens, Pad of Paper, Sand Timer

hungry Hippos clone with the opposite objecive. Plastic balls are released into the circular play area from a bucket above the board, the players have 2 levers each which they frantically move back and forth, which in turn moves the puppies arms, to prevent balls entering their puppies chute, The winner being the player with the least amount of balls, played over a series of 10 rounds the winner of each round reciving a bone, the player with the most bones after 10 rounds is declared the overall winner.

boardgame: The Penguins of Madagascar: Return to the Habitat
description:
An elite strike force with unmatched commando skills!  The Penguins of Madagascar have a mission, to get back to their habitat before the Zoo opens.  Help Rico, Kowalski, Private and Skipper get back to the Habitat and WIN!

For anyone who love the Penguins, this simple game is for you.  You play one of the four Penguins of Madagascar and your goal is to reach your habitat base as fast as you can.

The game curve is fast as it is geared to a younger generation, though is can be enjoyed by any age.  Ostensibly, this is a simple roll-and-move game, but the added aspects of the magnets in the board and the pieces adds an interesting variability not seen in many games.  The idea is a simple one: get to home base.  The difference is that there are several "turning" points on the board that alters you path of direction.  Either you move ahead, jump forward, or are send backwards.

Overall a game that can be played and enjoyed by all.

Adult version of the Bezzerwizzer+ family edition, the game is basically a trivia game with some new added elements.

The main differences between the normal trivia game and Bezzerwizzer+ Adult is that you have a xylofone and questions regarding picking the right tune, guessing the right song etc. Also there are questions regarding remembering differences on pictures, logic and numbers, but also normal trivia questions.

The game is played on a traditional Bezzerwizzer board, and the draw question mechanism ensures that previous versions of the game can be fully incorporated into this game also.

It can be played in up to four teams, consisting of as many players as you want, recommended is two-three per team.

boardgame: Barbie Race to the Party Game
description: Two to four players race to collect a dress, a tiara, shoes, and a gift. Money is collected at the ATM spaces. Each ATM card tells the owner at which store the card can be used.  The first to buy these things and get to the party wins.

Tego is a fast paced bidding and strategy game where a player competes with others 
in hiring 12 best characters with a limited amount of money and time they have.

The game play is approximatelt 30 mins, with 2-3 mins setup, and suitable for 2-6 players and for 
ages 8 and above.

Players will become Tego, a Human Resource Manager for Pablo the billionaire. Pablo has entrusted Tego to form
a Management Team that will run his new company, Pablo Chocos. With $50,000, players' task is to headhunt and 
hire 12 best characters within 10 working days (10 rounds). The player who emerges with the group of best 
characters and meets most of Pablo's criteria shall be the winner.

Out of these winners, only a few will be The Ultimate Headhunter. These are those who can score the maximum
110 points. So not all winners will become The Ultimate Headhunter, only the few great ones will.

The game play involves players bidding for these characters. One character will be bid at a time. The highest bidder gets the character. Players can also negotiate and trade during the game. The challenge is how one needs to achieve the target of the game without running out of money and also not to undervalue them until you lose
the bid. Thus one needs to bid wisely, value the characters at the right place and to get them at the right
time.

The Card Game comes with a package of 200 playing cards, 2 sets of 48 characters, and a fully coloured game 
manual.

Tego has also been designed for tournaments and competitions.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the publisher's website,

"From his throne in Asgard, the god Odin looked down upon Midgard, Alfheim and Nidavellir, the lands where humans, alfs and dvergar dwell. Many were the battles fought in these lands, and great were the feats of bravery and skill, but who among the mortal warriors should Odin send his Valkyries to claim? Only one more warband could be admitted to Valhalla, the great hall of warriors, before the doors were forever closed...

Valhalla's Gate pits warbands of eight or nine humans, elves or dwarves against each other in a race for the rune stone that will admit them to Valhalla. Designed for 25mm or 28mm miniatures, it features an easy-to-learn rules system that offers the option of either a one-night game or a full-on campaign, complete with character advancement.

The rules system includes shape-shifter characters, 25 different runic spells that add magic to the combat, a fast-paced, card-based initiative system, stats for 12 monsters drawn from Norse mythology, rules for inclement weather, and 25 different scenarios."

3-player only card game that is a mixture of trick-taking, bidding, bluffing, and melding. The leader can lead, single, pair, 3-of-the-kind, 4-of-the-kind, 5-of-the-kind or full-house. Other player must play the same number of cards face down but do not have to match the hand of the leader.

Then, from the leader, reveal the hand. You only reveal it if you can beat the previous player. Winner takes the point equal to the number of the cards.

Very unique game that requires good hand management.

Multi-player puzzle solving game using 7 dice. Your goal is to come up with the "answer", which is the sum of 2 black dice (11 to 66), by using other 5 white dice and arithmetic operations (add, subtract, multiply and divide). Whoever solved it first says "Jamaica!" and show the solution.

Make some noise - or not! Make the sounds of the animal cards you play, but don't be fooled by the silent cards! Start a donkey parade to catch the other players off guard, or skip someone's turn with a rooster card. The first player to play all their cards is the winner!

Game Play:
Play proceeds with each player able to discard as many cards as possible from their hand on their turn. The first card played on a turn must be either the same color or the same animal as the face up card on the top of the discard pile. The following cards played on a player's turn must be the same animal as the first card discarded. Players may keep playing as many cards that they have that match. When discarding a card, a player must make the sound of the animal that is played with the exception of certain special action card rules. When a player cannot discard any card from their hand on their turn, they must draw one card from the draw pile. Their turn ends immediately, so they cannot use that card until their next turn. The first player who successfully discards all of their cards wins.

WHAT THEY'LL LEARN: Colors, Observation, Animals

From the Discovery Bay website:

"In Literati Challenge, tall tales, side splitting yarns and hilarious verse are all part of the fun, but what’s best of all is you’ll be writing it! That’s right Literati Challenge is the new, laugh out loud party game of bluffing and creative chutzpa. Players get five words on cards. Each player must write a story using all the words in 90 seconds. The challenge? You may not know the meaning of the words, but to score points you need to make the other players believe you do. Players are scored during each turn. First player to reach 300 points wins. Literati Challenge is sure to get your creative mojo rising and your family and friends howling!"

This differs from Baffle Gab in that it is targeted for adults (Age 15+) where Baffle Gab is a Family game (Age 7+). The words used would be different and lend to a different experience. The most significant difference between these games is that in Literati Challenge, the cards provide word definitions on the back of the cards, while the cards in Baffle Gab do not.

A skill contest for two players.  Tussle has two enclosed marbles which roll over a target platform containing one hole in the center.  Each player grips a handle and both at the same time try to roll their color marble into the target hole.  The slightest movement will send the marbles rolling but since both players are moving their handles at the same time, it is difficult to tell which way they will roll.  Skill, rather than strength, counts most.  The player whose marble goes in the hole first, is the WINNER.

Players flip cards and try to score as many runs as they can before totaling three outs.  Basic baseball rules are followed.

The player with the most runs after 9 innings wins.

The cards include (total=38; 36 + 2 instruction cards):
Ball (10)
Strike (10)
Foul Ball (2)
Foul Out (1)
Fly Out (2)
Out (double play at second) (1)
Out (double play at first) (1)
Single (2)
Double (2)
Triple (1)
Home Run (1)
Hit by Pitcher (1)
Stolen Base (1)
Balk (1)

Includes:
8.5x11 page with baseball diamond

from the back of the box,

"Nine challenging battles for Warhammer 40,000

Two mighty Ork warlords, Ghazghkull and Nazdreg, have joined forces to invade the Imperial planet of Piscina IV. It's up to the 3rd Company of the Dark Angels, led by Master Beliel, to contain the Orks' intitial onslaught and reclaim the world from their savage greenskin foes. If the Dark Angels fail, the galaxy will quake before an Ork Waaagh of unimaginable power and ferocity.

Storm of Vengeance features a series of Warhammer 40,000 battles depicting the Dark Angels' defence of Piscina IV.

The Battles follow the course of the campaign and culminate in the Orks' final desperate attempt to capture the planet. The scenarios also include special rules and new characters for the campaign."

Contents:
-Scenario book
-Space Marines briefing booklet
-Orks briefing booklet
-Power plant card building
-Warp portal card template

Each player has thirteen hexagonal tiles, each of which has a unique arrangement of pointers to various edges. Tiles may be added to the playing area or moved in the direction of any of their pointers, possibly to stack on pieces of either colour which are then pinned. A player wins when the opponent's king (0-pointer) is captured.

Full rules: http://www.cameronius.com/games/stax

Several changes to the original rules have been applied during Nestorgames production process:

- 'Holed' tiles: So now it's allowed to pick up tiles with just 1 free side as you can insert one finger into the tile's hole. Moreover, you can see through the hole what's underneath (so you know which the hidden tiles are).

- New king: Now the king is the tile with no pointers. You cannot move your tiles unless your king is on the board. The king can move 1 space in any direction. The king is captured by removing it, so this prevents it from escaping by 'climbing'.

- The queen: The 6-pointer tile is the queen. The most powerful tile for attacking, but the weakest for defending!

- 3 player expansion: A 3rd set of tiles (blue) allows a 3rd player to play Stax.

boardgame: Quest: Zeit der Helden – Der Weihnachtsmann
description:
This mini-expansion consists of a new hero: Der Weihnachtsmann (Santa Claus).
It was presented as a free give-away at Christmas 2010 by Pegasus, the publisher.
See images for more information.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Erynie is a board game based on book under the same title by Marek Krajewski, a popular Polish crime fiction writer. Each player becomes an investigation officer, receives the main suspect accused of a crime and one innocent person. The goal is to find evidence of guilt or innocence.

Game is won by a player whose accusations are closest to the truth.

At the beginning of the game players receive randomly one suspect and one innocent person. During the game players are visiting several places in Lviv to collect evidence. After few turns of gathering proofs players can go to the police station and leave collected evidence there. Proofs have different influence on suspects and can be used as evidence of guilt or innocence. Players have to use their proofs to excuse their selected innocent person and to accuse their suspected person.

The classic match game with a twist!
Place all your Princess pieces on the game board spaces.  Flip over a card and try to find each of the Princesses' enchanted friends and objects! Collect the most Princess pieces and you win!!

Like a reversed "old Maid" you get rid of cards by getting pairs. You draw a card from your opponent, and if you got a pair you put it away. When you are out of cards you leave the game.  The player who has the "knerten" card at the end of the game is the winner.

The game is based on the movie "knerten", a movie based on a book by Anne-Cath Vestly. The story is about a boy who finds a friend in a wooden stick, who lives when the adults doesn’t look. (I guess the thought is that kids find joy in simple things. The irony is that now you can buy plastic “knerten” in the toy shops In Norway.

All illustrations are shots from the film.

Dogs, Hogs, Frogs & Logs is the first in a series of games featuring the original "3-in-1" Elementary Match System designed by Richard A. Ramirez.  In this system, players seek to collect cards by achieving any of the following match-types during their turn.


    Spell a character's name (i.e. spell DOG, HOG, FROG, or LOG)



    Add up to the number 10



    Match three character images in-a-row.


Whenever any of these matches is successfully made, the player who made the match will collect all the cards constituting the match.

The DHFL Official Game Guide explains the many ways in which the game can be played.

The Official Game Guide for Dogs, Hogs, Frogs & Logs can be accessed at 
http://www.thegamecrafter.com/games/dogs-hogs-frogs-amp-logs

boardgame: Spillet om Prinsessen som ingen kunne målbinde
description:
Based on a norwegian fairytale.

The players are searching for words that rhymes with the words that the prinsess have. The prinsess have five secret words that you can look at when you land on a prinsess spot. You collect words by landing on a "Askeladden" spot. (The hero of many norwegian tales). By landing on a king spot you can trade words.
family: Series: Fra meg til deg (Damm / Egmont)

boardgame: The Office Trivia Game: The Sequel
description:
Get ready for a new wave of fun with all new questions about the Scranton, Pennsylvania, branch of Dunder Miffin.

Based on the hit NBC sit-com "The Office", players move from cubical to cubical, collecting Dundie tokens along the way.

You'll hear people talking in the Parking Lot and have to guess who the quotes belong to.  In the Break Room all the people from the show, who don't work in Scranton, show up to give you some special trivia.  Most cards feature two levels of questions, Assistant to the Regional Manager and Regional Manager, so fans of all levels can play together.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Solid State War is a print-and-play game line set during a war of resources beginning in the year 2035 AD. It is a light, fast-playing game of aerial dogfights between small numbers of jets. The objective of play is to defeat your opponent's air forces in close combat. The game is designed to be suitable for quick pickup games as well as long term campaigns.

As a print-and-play game, players are expected to print out and prepare the playing pieces with household craft tools (scissors, hobby knife, and ordinary glue). These playing pieces include jet fighters, movement and shooting tools, unique dice and (as an option) 3D paper models.

Game sessions begin by choosing a scenario. Each scenario establishes the starting positions and objectives of the fighter jets on each team. There are two sides in the war, the Rebels and the State. The scenarios are sometimes written to favor one side, to reflect the strengths and weaknesses of the opposing armies.

Once a scenario is selected, the players spend a certain number of "army points" to purchase their aircraft and outfit them with more experienced crews, weapons and special devices. Each jet uses its own record sheet, and during play the player controlling that jet is responsible for marking its current damage, ammo and other variables.

Gameplay proceeds by rounds, with each jet taking a turn according to its initiative score. Movement is handled using a fan-shaped "movement key" and rolling dice to determine the maximum turning angle for that instance. Attacks are handled with a "range finder" tool that is placed in front of the attacking jet and used to determine if any targets can be fired upon. Dice are rolled to determine the severity of damage.

Scenarios may be won by destroying the enemy team's jets, or by disabling or disarming the enemy team, forcing a forfeit. Some scenarios have special victory conditions, such as taking recon photos or bombing a ground target.

After a scenario, both sides are awarded some army points to further develop their air forces or replace ruined equipment. These changes may be carried over for use in the next scenario.

The base release for this game is the Air War Starter Set PDF. It contains all of the essential components, such as print-and-play dice, maneuvering and range-finding tools, speed tokens, smoke clouds, bombing targets and 2D jet fighter game pieces. This set includes two versions of the RG-6 Tigress, a fighter jet used by both sides during the war. The custom dice include a unique distribution of numbers, as well as keywords that are used in different situations.

The game is designed to be supplemented with individual vehicle packs containing new options and game pieces, as well as 3D paper model PDFs that can be assembled and used in place of the standard 2D jet fighter pieces. Expansions are planned that will introduce new jet types, heroic crew members, campaigns, ground vehicles and ships.

"Return with us now to those thrilling days of yesteryear..."

In 1948 the Lone Ranger radio program made the following announcement, "In celebration of the 15th year of this program you can get your very own model of the Lone Ranger Frontier Town. With this offer you can follow the adventures of The Lone Ranger and Tonto."  That day, faithful fans of the masked man and his sidekick rushed grocery stores all across America buying up boxes of Cheerios.  On each the 9 boxes of cereal were printed paper card models depicting an exact likeness of the wide-open, wild and woolly western town made famous by the Lone Ranger and Tonto.  Four play maps featuring locations such as the Sawtooth Mountains, the Powderhorn River, the Overland Trail and yes even the Lone Ranger’s Secret hideout were also made available via mail-order.  Once all 71 pieces were assembled children could follow along with their favorite radio adventures or play 1 of these 8 exciting scenarios.

Game No. 1 - The Race for Life!

Game Start: Rimrock Bridge
Game End: Old Spanish Fort

Summary: While camping near Rimrock Bridge, the Lone Rnager overhears a band of bad-men plotting to ride to the Old Spanish Fort to give the commanding officer false information about an attack by Comanche Indians.
One player is the Lone Ranger, the other is the band of bad-men. First to reach the fort wins the game.

Game No. 2 - The Sheriff and the Rustlers

Game Start: Town Meetin’ House
Game End: Abandoned Silver Mine

Summary: Cattle Rustlers, after gathering near the Meetin’ House, plan to go into action against Ranchers near Lone Ranger Frontier Town. Sheriff “Two-Gun” Taylor discovers their plan and heads toward Abandoned Silver Mine, so that he can climb shaft and warn the Ranchers from this high tower. Rustlers try to reach the mine first to head off the Sheriff and stop the warning. One player is Sheriff Taylor. Other is gang of Rustlers. Winner is one who reaches mine first!

Game No. 3 - Save the Bridge!

Game Start: Red Valley Desert; Sam, the Guide’s House, and Range Rider’s Inn (For three players. If only two play, start from any two of above locations.)
Game End: Lone Pine Railroad Bridge.

Summary: Tonto sends up smoke signal from Red Valley Desert that a scheme is afoot to blow up the Lone Pine Bridge and wreak the Union Pacific Frontier Train. Signal is seen by Sam, the Guide, and a cowboy at Range Rider’s Inn. One player is Tonto, one is Sam the Guide and one is cowboy. All start riding toward the bridge. First there saves the bridge!

Game No. 4 - Find a Fortune!

Game Start: Hidden Valley.
Game End: Powderhorn Land Office.

Summary: Two or three desert rat prospectors (depending on whether 2 or 3 players are in the game) have discovered a rich vein of silver ore! Each has been working alone but by a strange coincidence all have stumbled on the vein at the same time… So all start heading for the Powderhorn Land Office at full speed in order to register their claim to the land.
The silver mine belongs to the one who reaches the Land Office first.

Game No. 5 - Stop the Hold-up!

Game Start: Sawtooth Mountains and Echo Canyon (One player from each location.)
Game End: Union Pacific Railroad Station.

Summary: The Lone Ranger is on a scouting expedition up in the Sawtooth Mountains. Tonto gallops up with news that a gang of hold-up men are riding down from Echo Canyon and mean to move in on the Railroad Station and hold up all the passengers on the in bound train as it stops in Lone Ranger Frontier Town!
One player is the Lone Ranger and the other is the gang of “toughs.” Does the Lone Ranger reach the station in time to prevent the robbery? Play the game and see!

Game No. 6 - The Big Rodeo

Game Start: Old Cattle Trail Bridge and Outpost General Store. (One player from each location.)
Game End: Lazy “Y” Ranch Corral.

Summary: The Lazy “Y” is holding a big wild-west rodeo for the entertainment of the citizens of Lone Ranger Frontier Town. One of the big events is a match race for two riders – one to start at the bridge and the other from the General Store.
First rider to reach the corral is to win one of the finest cow-ponies in the Lazy “Y” string!

Game No. 7 - Hidden Gold!

Game Start: Adobe Village and Southwest Bugle. (One player from each location).
Game End: Rainbow’s End Island.

Summary: A notorious outlaw steals a precious old Treasure Map from a young Indian brave who lives in the Adobe Village. This map shows location of priceless old Indian relics buried for safekeeping on Rainbow’s End Island. A reporter for the Southwest Bugle hears about the theft and determines to beat the outlaw to the island and surprise him there!
One player is reporter who starts from the “Bugle” while the other, the outlaw, starts from the Adobe Village. First player at the Island wins the game and the Treasure!

Game No. 8 - Kidnapper’s Hide-out

Game Start: Alkali Ike’s Log Cabin, Mother Williard’s Rooming House and Rancher’s National Bank. (For three players, each to start from one of these three locations…If only two play, start from any two of the three  locations.)
Game End: Rustler’s Cave.

Summary: Lone Ranger Frontier Town is startled one sunny morning when Wash Perkins, who owns the Grist Mill, discovers that Sheriff Taylor’s Indians are holding Kate captive in Rustler’s Cave for Ten thousand dollars ransom.
Wash sounds the alarm, and the first to rush to the rescue are old Alkali Ike, a cowboy staying at the rooming house, and a cashier from the bank.
One player is Alkali Ike, one is the cowboy, and one is the bank cashier. The glory for rescuing Kate Taylor goes to the one who arrives at Rustler’s Cave first!

HOW TO PLAY (General instructions printed on back of each map section)

1. Punch out game markers and circular playing discs. Place playing discs in container (such as a cup), shake well and allow each player to select one disc in turn. Each player then moves his game marker according to the directions on the disc he draws. Of course, no player may look at disc before selecting it.

2. Start each game on spot of Ground Plan colored GREEN, which is nearest the starting location indicated for that particular game...And move toward goal on circular stepping stones printed on Ground plan.

3. End each game on spot of Ground plan colored RED, which is nearest to ending location for that particular game.

4. Playing discs marked “Overtake player ahead” does not apply in all games, since some players in some games will take entirely different routes to the goal. If you are playing a game in which this disc does not apply, either remove it from play or give person who draws it another turn.

5. It is up to each player to figure out shortest route to his destination.

6. If you need more game markers for extra players simply cut them out of plain cardboard and color with pencil or crayon.

A word building game for players to form words from a common pool of letter tiles.   The pool begins with three blindly drawn letters. Subsequent letters are similarly drawn one at a time. At any time a player may call out a word that can be formed from the available letters. Upon making a word it is placed in the scoring area of the respective player. Additional words can be formed either by using only letters from the pool, or by poaching one or more letters from the pool of an extant word in another player's scoring area. If a player uses another player's word, that word is taken and given to the player forming the new word. When no new words can be made, each player scores the words in their area. A word's score is equal to the number of letters in the word minus one.

The ideal compact football game for the All-American armchair quarterback. An ideal game for families and parties. Can be played by 2 or 4 players.

This game has been especially designed to give "Armchair Quarterbacks"the opportunity to try their own football strategy. It is not a game of chance but creates realistic game situations in which the play outcomes depend on which player outwits his opponent.

The fold-out magnetic play board includes easy-to-read and easy-to-use scoreboards, time and play clock, indicators for "yards to go", times out, wind factors, downs and quarter, and a scale model for the actual length of a football field with each yard line marked for recording of exact yardage. magnetic markers are used as the "first down marker" by placing it 10 yards in front of the ball when there's a first down.

The spinner-play selector cards are used to select play calls and punts, field goal attempts, extra points, fumble recoveries, penalties, length of kickoff returns, onside kicks and wind conditions.

There are 42 cards used for running plays including penalties, fumbles and injuries. There are 42 pass play cards including interceptions, fumbles and penalties. The pass completion rate is nearly 50%, with an average completion gain of over 11 yards and includes possibility for touchdowns. There are over 300 different outcomes possible from the use of these 84 cards.

Nearly total realism can be simulated by advanced players. but the game is enjoyable by players of all levels.

From the box:

Get set for some brilliant teamwork as Wallace and Gromit embark on their latest career move!  In this cracking game, teams of players compete to create items from modelling dough, whilst the others try to guess what's being made.  As this grand adventure continues, players can steal items as they move around the board and try to avoid Preston as he seeks to steal points.  The team who model and guess the most items will triumph as champion creators!

Description:

Teams take turns to roll-and-move their dough-modelled playing piece round the board, either modelling inventions out of dough or stealing inventions from another team.  Inventions are graded as hard (red / 3 points), medium (yellow / 2 points) or easy (green / 1 point).

On a modelling turn, the modeller makes as many as he can from that colour in one minute.  Each correctly-guessed invention moves the teams scoring piece along the scoring track by the appropriate number of spaces - first to 30 wins - and the team keep the card.

On a stealing turn, the team take a card from another team and advance that many spaces, while the other team goes back that many spaces, for a total swing of 2, 4 or 6 points.

After each turn, the team also roll-and-move the Preston piece either way round the board.  If it lands on another team, or another team land on it, that teams discards a card and loses the appropriate amount of points.

A variation listed in the rules is that dough cannot be reused during a turn, limiting the size and number of models that can be made.

Game contents:

Game board
200 item cards
3 pots of modelling dough
3 modelling mats
Preston playing piece
3 playing pieces
Sand timer
Dice
Instructions

This is self-published strategy level wargame based on Japanese TV animation "Mobile suit Z Gundam" & "Mobile suit Gundam ZZ". This game cover three-way struggle with Titans, AEUG and Axis Zeon.

A player manage a treasury, assigns it in the development of the weapon, and the military operations.
A game purpose is control of important cities more than enemy factions at the end of the game.
The turn order of the military operations depends on a chit-pull system. The performance of the weapon and pilot's and captain's ability influence a battle greatly.

boardgame: Brain Race: La grande sfida delle menti
description:
A big race for 2/4 players or team, based on memory, logic, math and word quizzes. Designed by Francesco Berardi, quizzes by Ennio Peres - one of the most famous game experts in Italy, author of many books about logic and math games. Some strategy in choosing the questions at each turn is important to get the most points out of them. Sometime it is also possible to bet on the opponets' ability to solve a certain quiz.
A manual by Peres about the secrets of the mind and how to train it is also included.

The game represents the combat between various Factions of psychic Operatives.  Loosely based on the 2009 movie: Push.

You win if at the end of your turn you control the center of the board. Control of the center represents the fulfillment of your own Master Plot and the foiling of the plots of the opposing Factions.

The Board is an 11 x 11 Square grid.  The 4 spaces in each corner are called Faction Boxes.  The Center square is key to winning.  Players win by controlling four of the adjacent spaces to the center: either the four orthogonal or four diagonal spaces around the center space.  Operatives, represented by counters, occupy squares, and have various psychic special abilities that affect movement and attack modes.

FLAPS is like an extreme version of UNO. Flaps is a shedding-type card game for two or more players that is popular in the United Kingdom and Czech Republic. The sole aim of Flaps is to discard all of the cards in one's hand; the first player to play his final card, and ergo have no cards left, wins the game.

The game uses a custom deck of cards written in both English and Czech. The game has seven levels, each level adding new functionality.

"Zulu, Battle of Isandhlwana" is a game that simulates the battle between British imperial forces and Zulu warriors on January 22nd, 1879 in Zululand, and area northeast of what is today the Republic of South Africa.  This battle is known as the worst disaster suffered by colonial troops in a confrontation against native forces.

This DTP publication by Luis Carreño Joicey, deals with the history of the battle and includes a detailed simulation of the conflict which will help to better understand what happened.

One player plays the with the British side and the other with the Zulu side, if there are two players. Additional players may take command of individual Zulu formations (the "horns", "chest" and "loins").

The basic game serves only to as an introduction and to let players familiarize themselves with with the rules; players should use the advanced rules for a more complete game.

The object of Glitter Gulch is to win one million dollars gambling.  Player landing upon a casino play roulette or chuck-o-luck.  Players not gambling on a given turn may make side bets with one another.

Nahua is an solo dice game of the Aztec civilization.

In this game you take on the role of head priest of Tenochtitlan, jewel of the cities of the Nahua. You must appease the god of the darkness with sacrifices while making sure that your civilization grows and flourishes. There are 20 rounds to the game representing the daynames of the Aztec calendar cycle, and to win you must offer up 26 sacrificial hearts or the sun will not rise again and the world will end.

You begin the game with 3 harvest dice (white) and 2 city dice (yellow). Later you will have access to four additional expansion dice. These dice show a variety of resource icons:

corn, useful to harvest to feed your people, extra corn can be stored from one round to the next

black corn, which you may not harvest, and can mean famine and death if have 3 or more ears of it

peasants, who can multiply your harvest yield with their hard work

priests, who can cause your civilization to expand

hearts, with which you must to appease Tezacatipoca

warriors, who can gain you captured slaves for your sacrifices

gods, which come with expansion dice, who can give you divine bonus resources

During the game you must keep track of corn and hearts as well as rounds.

Play:
There are 3 rolls allowed per round, and you must lock a die each roll, re-rolling any others you choose; with the exception of black corn which is always automatically locked on a roll (i.e. you are not allowed to re-roll a die showing the black corn icon, unless there is divine intervention). Some dice have 2 icons on a face; in this case both roll resources showing can be used. You must harvest enough corn in each round to feed all city and expansion dice being rolled (i.e. ears of corn=number of city and expansion dice rolled). At the start of the game this means you must harvest 2 ears of corn for each round, one for each of the city dice. As your civilization grows and you gain expansion dice, the number of ears of corn required will grow as well. If you do not have enough ears of corn to equal your rolled city and expansion dice (and you must use any stored corn you have as part of this), you must subtract 1 heart from your total (corn harvest may be saved from round to round).

During the game you may gain additional expansion dice. These dice are a darker orange and have a set of black dots (1 to 4 dots) on one face and the great jaguar god is displayed in white. You gain these expansion dice in numerical order and they will give you extra peasants, priests, corn to harvest, warriors and hearts as well as gods to pray to.

Game End: 
If after 20 rounds you have offered up 26 sacrificial hearts you have won and the world is safe for another calendar cycle.

boardgame: Kirche, Glaube & Reformation (fan expansion for Catan: Cities and Knights)
description:
Unofficial fan expansion of Catan: Cities & Knights.

Adds several new aspects to Catan: Cities and Knights.

Introduces an Inquisitor token, Religion Development Calendars, Religion Progress card set, Bread commodity cards and Beer commodity cards.  While Bread is analogous to Grain (the way Coin is analogous to Ore in Cities and Knights), Beer is a commodity that can only be produced on a Grain intersection when a new Diocese building is present.  Beer is used to create Beer Festivals which affect an opponent's Road, Ship, Settlement or City for a period of time; this action can lower the Victory Point total for that opponent!

The third edition of Kirche, Glaube & Reformation ("Church, Faith and Reformation") also incorporates another unofficial fan expansion, Hexen, Zauberer & Drachen (fan expansion for Catan: Cities and Knights) ("Witches, Wizards & Dragons"), which introduces a Wizard token, Magic Development Calendars, Magic Progress card set and Magic commodity cards.  Magic commodities can only be collected on a brick intersection when a new Citadel building is present.

This combined version requires a custom 12 sided event die (6 barbarian ships and 6 city gates, the last of which is a "wild card" gate allowing the player who rolled the dice to choose the color gate).

If played separately, each fan expansion requires a custom 8 or 10 sided event side (4 barbarian ships and 4 city gates or 5 barbarian ships and 5 city gates, the last of which is a "wild card" gate).

Adds tactical complexity to the game and game length.

Belongs to the Catan Series.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
(from Histoire & Collections webpage:)

en français:
De Cao-Bang à Kaboul, ou DCK, est une règle d’escarmouche moderne qui vous permettra de rejouer tous les engagements militaires à petite échelle depuis la fin de la seconde guerre mondiale jusqu’à nos jours.



(BGG user translation and description:)
  
In English :
De Cao-Bang à Kaboul (or DCK) - from Cao-Bang to Kabul - is a modern skirmish rule which will allow you to replay all small scale military engagements from the end of the WWII to our days. 
 
The players take each one a group of infantry and command squads which can have vehicle support (some 10 miniatures each in the smallest scenarios).

The objectives of the game are decided by the same players or can be used those from the scenarios which came with the rulebook
The game uses a classic I go, you go turn sequence which have 4 sub-phases for each player: 
  a) rally
  b) localization of the enemy
  c) movement or fire
  d) melee.   
There is also an advance rules section to add optional elements to the main rules.
 IN FRENCH ONLY.

A game for up to 5 children.

It is an interactive race game where the player whose mouse gets into the house first is the winner.

There is not dice in this game. Each player gets one mouse and a counter. There is also a cat which is used to mark a different color each turn.

The players take turns to guess in which hand other players hide the counters. If they guess right, they move their mouse to the next color space that matches the color marked by the cat. If they guess wrong, then it is the challenged player who advances the mouse in the same way.

If the cat catches a slow mouse, gues what! the slow mouse is not penalized, instead it gets to advance 3 spaces. A quick, fun, and easy game for 4 years-old upwards.
A version of the classic game Monopoly, with names and images of board elements taken from York, England.

Cats and Mice run around the board in a crazy race to save their skins (box text).

Funny game in which each player must move his mice to the finish line, while at the same time tries to get the more the better of the other players mice.
The winner is the player with most points at the end of the game (from the game rules).

Gatos y ratones corren por el tablero en una loca carrera para salvar el pellejo (box text)

Juego muy divertido en que cada jugador debe avanzar sus ratones hasta la meta, mientras intenta comer el máximo de ratones de los otros jugadores.
El ganador es quién consigue más puntos al finalizar el juego.

Hungry Dinosaurs roam through their territories searching for food. But if the little dinosaurs are too greedy, the dangerous T-Rex takes his chance. Who gets the biggest pray in the end? A card game where you shouldn't be greedy.

This is a push-your-luck game. 
First you gamble with your 2 dino-cards where you will look for food (in 2 out of 3 places). Luck then decides in what 2 places you can roam for food. On your turn you open the cards finding food. You can turn cards as long as you want. But there are T-Rex in the cards to and if you draw one you loose all the food you found. So try to gather enough but don't push your luck.

Components:
3 territory tokens
3 territory cards
8 dinosaur cards
60 pray cards
The game is part of the T-RexWorld line of Die Spiegelburg.

boardgame: Bx Rock, Paper, Scissors, Fire and Water
description: Bx Rock, Paper, Scissors, Fire and Water (BxRPSFW) is an abstract strategy board game for 2 players. Each of the 2 players takes turns moving one of his/her 17 game pieces which include Rock, Paper, Scissors, Fire and Water. Rock captures Scissors, Scissors captures Paper, and Paper captures Rock. In addition to this, Fire may capture any of Rock, Paper, or Scissors. Any of Rock, Paper, or Scissors may capture Water. And Water captures only Fire. No like pieces may capture each other. The object of the game is to be the first player to capture the opposing flag, which may not move at all.

Napoleon's Final Campaign: Germany, 4-6 September, 1813

Napoleonic 20 #11 (Germany 20 #3) Expansion Kit

Designer Lance McMillan offers a fascinating Expansion Kit for Grossbeeren 20, placing you in command of either the Coalition forces or the French army. The French forces are small, but nimble, pitted against their larger opponent. As at Grossbeeren, the Allied army's initial deployment is its greatest weakness – if the French can move quickly they can over-whelm the Prussians and occupy the Juterbog objective before the rest of the Allied army can be brought to bear. But can the French keep Ney in command of an army rather than personally leading a charge?

NOTE, Dennewitz 20 is not a ‘stand alone’ game. You must own a copy of Grossbeeren 20 to use this expansion kit.

Sword & Shield is a tactical game of fantasy combat for 2 or more players. Using playing cards, figures, and a chess or checkers board you can fight out gritty fantasy battles.

The heart of the system lies in using a standard deck of 54 cards.  These govern your ability to move and fight.  Your ability to play the cards to your advantage will determine victory more so than simple luck of the draw.

Sword & Shield combines card drawing, tactical card play, and fantasy miniatures maneuvering into a simple 2 pages of rules!

This first release contains: Humans, Elves, Dwarves, Orcs Goblins, Giant Spiders, Skeletons, Zombies, Lizardmen and the dreaded Cthull all await your presence in the arena!

Pick up your copy of Sword & Shield, and let the battle begin!

The map shows the current trend and changed its status by playing some cards.  This trend strongly affects the victory condition.

Similar to Monopoly but with added elements.  Players can take out loans and there are opportunities to "cheat" the other players or raid their properties.  You roll and move around a board like Monopoly and land on businesses, but there is also a secondary track called the high-roller track.

Using the detailed street map of Munich (München), which is set out in four colors, each player is given five companies to be established in the city as close to the center as possible.

With the help of dice and counters, each players chooses where to place, turn or remove company tiles.

"It's the word in-between that counts!  Beat the clock... and double your score."

A party-style game of finding a word to fit between two others, that forms the second half of a common two-word phrase with the first word and the first words of a common two-word phrase with the second word.

The box includes: 125 triangular plastic tiles in 5 colours with values 1 to 5; 64 cardboard goal tiles (8 x 6 different types of increasing values); 5 dice (one for each color with numbers 1 to 5 and one star) and 4 game boards, one for each player.
Each of the game boards shows 3 empty pentagons (composed of 5 triangles each).

At the beginning of the game each player chooses 3 goal tile and puts them on his board under the empty pentagons..
At his turn the player throw the dice, gets the correspoding triangular tiles and places them on his board trying to complete his goals, Unused tile are placed back in the reserve.
When one goal is reached the player keeps the goal tile, gives back the triangles and takes a new goal tile.
The game cannot end before someone has collected at least five goal tiles.

Goal of the game is to score more points than the other players.

Track & Field Math is a game for children in grades 6 to 8 that uses a sports theme. It can be used to review for tests, brush up on a specific topic, or just for family fun.

There are eight "events" like the 100 meter dash, the pole vault, or the discus. Players roll dice to participate in the events and the first player to finish must answer the math question. Correct answers give the player a medal. The contestant with the highest medal total is declared the winner.

Includes a colorful board, 4 pawns, 120 question cards, a die, rules, score sheet, and a storage insert.

From the rules sheet:

A pocket word dice game consisting of seven white letter dice and one black symbol dice. Comes in a drawstring bag.

Each player takes a turn to shake and throw the eight dice. Using the letters on the upturned faces of the seven white letter dice, the aim is to make the highest score possible by creating one or more words of two or more letters. Players decide the number of rounds they wish to play in each game.

Each dice has a score value and these are added together to get a total for the turn. The blank has no score value and may be used as any desired letter.

If all the letters can be used in making two or three words, an extra 5 points is added to the player's score for that turn.

Six letter words add 15 points to the player's score.

Seven letter words add 25 points to the player's score.

The black symbol dice affects the player's throw by adding or subtracting points, imposing a time limit, allowing a rethrow of all dice, prohibiting multiple words or adding an extra blank.

description:
This Monopoly variant was published as a fundraiser for the Taft High School (Lincoln City, OR) 2009 Senior Class. The board features local businesses from Otis, Neotsu, Lincoln City, Glenden Beach, Lincoln Beach, and Depoe Bay instead of the traditional properties.

Houses and Hotels are replaced with green and red 'customer' chips. Chance and Community Chest cards are renamed as Fate and Beachcombing cards. Each turn player either draw transportation card(s) or move the player marker around the map to score. A collection of location cards will also provide bonus points at the end of the game. description:
A wargame in French published in 1982.

It's about the general Patton and the 3rd US army liberating Metz on the way to attack the Saarland.

It's a small scale little wargame with hex and counters. It's based on usual rules from that time: zones of control, communication lines, combat table based on attack vs defense ratio. 
One hex is for 3 km - 2 miles.
There are 130 counters and 111 represent battalions, regiments or "Combat Command".
The main goal is to cross the German defense - the west wall - behind the Saar river.

It was available in a ziplock bag.
The counters are mounted on a thin cardboard.
The map quality is good - but of course looking like other games from the 80s.

"El cetro de Yarek" ("Yarek's Sceptre") is a game of terror for players Ages 9 and up, and for 2 to 4 players. "El cetro de Yarek" was published in Spain at the end of the 1980's decade by the CEFA company specializing in party games with horror ambientation.

The game comes with:

- A 3D board game (including a thunder-roulette and two rotary discs)
- 4 magicians 
- 4 plastic bases
- 24 magic cards 
- 13 keys (9 false keys, 4 true keys)

Theme. The four most famous magicians of the world (Mr. Drake, Vudu, Fu-Manchu, Houdini) struggle hard to get the Sceptre's Yarek. The holder of the Sceptre will become the most powerful magician in the world. However the Sceptre is hidden in a phantasmagoric ship crowded by zombies.

Escape to Wisconsin Trivia is a roll and move trivia game with many similarities to Trivial Pursuit. Players take turns moving their tokens around the board on a track that is shaped like the outline of the state of Wisconsin. Questions are color coded into five categories, each question having to do with some aspect of the history and culture of Wisconsin: Famous People, Sports/Recreation, Geography, History/Government, and Agriculture/Industry. The winner of the game is the player who is the first to collect a scoring piece from each of the five colors. Scoring pieces are obtained by landing on star spaces and correctly answering a question corresponding to the color of the space.

description:
In Animal Shuffle, players are trying to gather a set of animal fluffy toys from a multiplayer claw catcher. On their turn, each player can move to one of a number of specified spaces on the 9x9 grid, so as to catch the hidden prize that lies there.

Players only know the color of their catch, and not its nature (rank). They can also only keep 6 prizes in their hand, so that they must choose which ones to keep to be able to build a valuable (poker-like) collection.

Each move must lead to a catch, and the game ends when a player can't make a legal move. The player with the best set in hand wins the game.

The Dutch game association Ducosim publishes the game magazine Spel!. With this magazine they will publish several 'Spel!enkaarten' (gamecards). These are games which fit on a A5 card. #0004 is 'Survival' and was included with Spel! 14.2 December 2010

In this game one player controls 6 wolves, and the other player controls 1 reindeer. The wolves try to surround the reindeer in such a way that it cannot move in any direction. The reindeer tries to escape the wolves and run towards the safety of the forest.

The reindeer moves in random directions, but the player can decide how far the reindeer moves in the given direction. The wolves are restricted to one square either horizontal or diagonal.

Jets is a science fiction racing game that does not use a game board, instead you play on a very large surface (for instance your carpet). Each player receives one jet-racer. Your jet is moved by placing a distance token which varies in length according to your set speed in front of your jet.

Cards are supplied to improve the special abilities of your personal jet.

A family travel game around the world. Players have to visit 6 mandatory cities plus some secondary ones. The first player to arrive in each of the mandatory cities must wait two turns, the rest of the players don't but have to visit some of the secondary cities related to that mandatory one (for example La Havana is the first secondary city of New York).
There are 3 means of transportation: 
  -road, the player always move but very slowly
  -boat, must roll 3+ with a dice
  -plane, must roll 6+ with two dice, but each move is further.

When all players have visited all mandatory cities, they must return to their start city in Spain. 
The cities give points to the player (mandatory cities more, secondary less). At the end the player with the most points win the game.

The popular card game gets a modern makeover with bold graphics and a sleek, portable case, making it a great gift idea! It's still the same classic game - but has been enhanced with an exclusive MOD die. For ages 7+.

--

Each player is dealt a stockpile of 30 cards. The winner will be whoever manages to empty their stockpile first. Cards are played onto four shared building piles in numerical sequence from 1 to 12. On each turn, a player draws until they have five cards in their hand, and then plays cards from either their hand, a top card from their discard piles, or their stockpile. At the end of their turn, a player must discard onto one of their four personal discard piles.

Included in this version is the new MOD die. At the start of your turn roll the die. Three sides depict numbers that you can use the dice in place of that numbered card. One side is a Skip-Bo you can use as a wild. One side lets you discard two cards on your turn instead of one and the last side makes your opponent draw one card to add to their stockpile.
