description:
Excerpted from the back of the book

Origins of the General War!

Featured Scenarios: Kzinti and Lyran cruisers square off in Cold Front. Orions rescue Hamilcar's son from the Klingons. WYN and Kzinti frigates battle on the edge of the radiation zone. Klingons ambush two Fed cruisers, and discovers on is a CX! Two Gorn X-ships pursue a Romulan ThunderHawk. An ISC squadron pursues the Hydrans into the rings.

Also includes... Cold Front (a history of the Coalition, the Kzintis, & the Hydrans); Universe (information about Doomsday edition & Prime Directive RPG); Tactics; Federation & Empire Q&A; two new X-Ship SSDs; and more.

Boy Crazy is a simple collectible card game for girls, created by Decipher Inc. during the Pokémon Trading Card Game boom.

Cards are distributed in packs of 9 cards. Each card featured a picture of a boy aged 12–22, his first name, set of statistics about him (height, date of birth, color of eyes), and a list of his likes and dislikes. Cards includes boys from across the United States, from different ethnic backgrounds and hair and eye colors, including disabled boys.

Game is basically a very simple matchmaking game. Minimum requirements for game are at least 9 cards, and each player should have pencil and paper. Play begins when players decide who gets to go first. Using the Boy Crazy cards (9 cards or more), first player secretly writes down which boy she likes best and why. Then the first player lays out the cards so everyone can see them. The other players try to make a match by writing who they think is the best boy for the first player and why. Then, each of the other players take turns revealing who they chose as her match. After the first player reveals her response, scores are tabulated for that round. Each player who correctly matches the first player's response scores a point. Play continues until every girl has a chance for her friends to guess her match. To start a new round players should discard starting set of 9 cards and draw a new set of 9 cards. The player or players who makes the most correct guesses wins.

The game was so popular that Decipher launched a companion "Boy Crazy" magazine in September 2001.

From the back of the cover:

Road to Baghdad gives Force on Force: Modern Wargaming Rules players all the information they need to re-fight the 2003 Allied Invasion of Iraq. From deadly little firefights between Iraqi Fedayeen and US Special Forces to armored strikes against Republican Guard positions across the Tigris River, Road to Baghdad offers a wealth of varied scenarios and mission objectives that will challenge the tactical abilities of new and experienced wargamers alike. With a wealth of new information to expand Force on Force: Modern Wargaming Rules, including detailed historical background and orders of battle for US, British, and Iraqi forces during the invasion, Road to baghdad allows players to really get to grips with the experience of war in the opening stages of Operation Iraqi Freedom.

A trick-taking game for traditional playing cards. Features 'boasting' - a push-your-luck mechanic.

Each round, an auction is held for the right to be 'big fish', who will have a better hand and should win more tricks than any little fish. Unlike other card games, you bid points of your score - being a big fish carries a greater element of risk, since you need to regain at least the points you just lost in the auction.

A game lasts around 4-6 rounds, decided in advance. For 2 players, 4 rounds are recommended.

summary of a round:
- DEAL 6 cards to each player. Deal out a face-up 'dummy hand' of 6 cards.

- Have an AUCTION for the right to be big fish. The winning bid is deducted from the big fish's score.

- BIG FISH declares the trump suit, then exchanges any number of cards from their hand with cards from the extra hand.

- Each player BOASTS how many tricks they will win.

- Starting with Big Fish, PLAY out the 6 tricks. There is no need to follow suit and the first-played suit has no special significance.

- Players who took fewer tricks than their boast win nothing. Players who win at least as many as their boast SCORE points equal to their boast squared.

Dealin' Damage adds a new level of detail to Outrider's damage track. Instead of just losing Armor Tokens, players can lose something crucial with each hit suffered. Tires blowout, engines sputter and stall, weapons jam and ultimately fail and your armor could shear off - taking parts of your car with it! Even the vehicle's driver isn't safe: one bullet could injure or outright kill the driver. 
  
The Dealin' Damage Bolt-On contains rules, damage cards, targeted damage cards, critical effect cards as well as a set of smoke and debris markers to enhance gameplay.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This puzzle puts brain versus brain in the spot light. All around the red sun! In this game, two players must face off with each other. Each gets two pegs to use. Players take turn moving a peg from one of the holes to the other. The first player to make it so the other cannot move loses and will not get the glory of the red sun! It is a think quick act quick game. The rules do not allow for time to think and strategize. You must be on your toes and always think one step ahead!

Publisher Blurb:

Member of the prestigious Testarossa dynasty, one the most influential noble houses of Argos, the rebellious and stubborn Alis was always the source of problems for all her family because of her impetuous behaviour. When she was thirteen Alis began to manifest supernatural talents. Her parents, fearful of Alis bringing shame to the family, used their influence to send her to the church. There, Alis developed her skills under the supervision of the Knights of the Saint Order. When she was only 21 obtained the title of High Captain in recognition of her extraordinary abilities.

Thanks to her control over energy and her capability to manifest wings of light and soar upon the skies, she has become a legend of the Knights of the Saint Order, and some see her as a angel sent by heaven.

Warrior - Church - Level 40

Wings of Light: If Alis sacrifices 3 LP during the Maintenance Phase, she obtains the Flying skill during the rest of the turn.

Valkyrie (Ki, Charte) - +2 Attack / +2 Damage Alis must be Flying.

Aegis' Wind (Ki, Movement, Escape) - Alis automatically escapes Hand-to-Hand combat and may move up to 12 inches.  This does not count as movement for the current turn.  She may not engage in hand-to-hand combat using this movement action.  Alis must be Flying.

Aegis (Ki, Attack) - +2 Attack / +2 Damage.  If the attack causes damage, retire all Positive States from the enemy unit.

Beyond the Winds (KI, Charge) - Retire Alis from the board.  During her activation in the following turn, place her in contact with any enemy unit in game and complete the Charge.  Alis returns to the board with 0 action points.  Alis must be Flying in the moment she uses this skill.

This pewter figure is unpainted and unassembled. Requires additional Anima Tactics figures to play.

Players herd their sheep around the board to collect Clover Tiles and also score bonus points for completing sentences.  This game teaches children educational skills such as forming basic sentences and completing sets.  The game play involves light strategy and also allows children to practice early reading and simple math.

Publisher's Description:
A Grammar Game Your Family will Flock To

Collect the clovers with your sheep to form sentences. Score with each subject, verb, adjective, and object. You’ll laugh at the wacky sentences you create!

WHAT THEY'LL LEARN: Early Vocabulary, Strategy, Basic Sentence Structure

Publisher Blurb:

Few agents of Samael have proven as useful to the organization as the Sylvain Janiel Ul Del Valiem. Young even by the standards of her race, Janiel is both an incomparable spell caster and an unequaled martial artist in the service of the Fallen Angel Dinah. Her mystical abilities allow her to invoke into the world a guardian spirit named Enjeil, who takes the form of an enormous eagle made of energy, through which she manifests her powers. Any obscure power that underestimates her will meet its end in a flurry of feathers.

Warrior / Mystic - Samael - Level 40

Einjeil Eyes (Magic, Seek) - This ability gives +8 inches to Janiel's Control Zone and +1 to the success roll of the Seek Control.

The Mark of Einjeil (Magic, Effect) - Choose an enemy unit within 20 inches.  Until the end of the turn, all Ranged Attacks of any friendly unit against that enemy gain +2 Attack.  Effect 16.

Feathers Storm (Magic, Ranged Attack) - Distance (20 Inches), +2 Damage.

Feathers Slash (Ki, Attack) - +2 Attack / +2 Damage.

Healing Breath (Magic, Effect) - A friendly unit inside Janiel's Control Zone gains Lv 2 Healing.

This pewter figure is unpainted and unassembled. Requires additional Anima Tactics figures to play.

From the publisher:
Wizkids is the DVD board game that teases your brain. Wizkids is about science and technology with bizarre trivia and experiments. Wizkids is fast, explosive, varied and enormously interesting.

Challenge each other in five different categories and train your brain. The DVD gives you and your fellow players directions in the form of films, photos and animations about the most unusual subjects. With more than 800 extraordinary trivia!

Do you have the brains of a prawn or are you much smarter that the rest? Do you know more? Can you calculate faster, draw better and remember or recognize more? than you will certainly win the game!

BATTLELINES is a collectible card game based on LEGO miniatures. However using miniatures is not required for game play.

Each Character card represents a miniature of miniFig World. Other cards types include Equipment and Weapons.

The game play is simple and battles are resolved with a dice D6. It is a 2 player game, with a customizable deck.
 BATTLELINES uses a unique system to keep track of Characters Ammo and Health using LEGO studs attached to each Character card.

Legendary miniFig Characters like "Brodie SARGE Munro" battle against each other using their Power (PWR) and Defence (DEF) and whatever Equipment or Weapons are available to beat their opponents.

miniFig World is an Australian based internet business which specialises in LEGO miniatures and accessories.

A simple race game from the early 50's. Players are trying to get to the Station first. Throw a die and move. You must obey the instructions on the square that you land on. For example, A Flat Tyre on space 44, which makes you go back to 33. Or a Green Traffic Light on space 16, which means you can quickly proceed to 21.
Traditional rules such as 'Have another turn if you throw a 6' and 'You need the exact throw to finish'.
A nice touch is the provision of a box to keep your pieces in. The pieces are 4 cars and a die.
artists: Eelco Martinus ten Harmsen van der Beek

description:
Hoksotin is a game where you spin a Twister-type spinner, that tells you what you'll need to find from the board, for example you'll have to find "Something round". In limited time you'll try and find as many round things as possible, marking the ones you already found with sticky little chips in you color. When the time runs out the chips are removed and correct ones are scored on your personal scoreboard. The one that gets most points in the limited number of rounds is the winner. There are 3 different two-sided boards with the art that is very appealing to the children.

Hoksotin was one of the finalists in the "Vuoden perhepeli 2010" -competion. (The Best Family Game of 2010).

A game which starts the same way but then the board tiles shift and each game is now unique.
'For 2 to 4 Adventurers' say the rules. You are dealt 3 room Cards and it is your objective to 'dynamite' each room so named. If you do this first and exit the board, you win. Each tile has a two digit number. You roll the dice to move and you must move the full throw (if you can't, you forfeit the throw) and you are allowed to change direction once. After moving, you check the pips on both dice, and this will relate to two tiles. Example; You roll a 5 and a 3, this will relate to tile 53 or tile 35, your choice. If you roll doubles, only one tile will be available. The tile you choose is picked up and placed somewhere else. It can be anyway round and it must be adjacent to an existing tile.There are special tiles in the game, for example, the Start tile or The Misty Mists tile.

Zuniq is an abstract game for two players, played in an 8x8 grid of evenly spaced dots.
Players take turns joining two horizontally or vertically adjacent dots by a segment. Eventually, closed zones will form. It’s irrelevant which player closes which zone; zones do not belong to any player.

There are only two restrictions:
- Once closed, zones are out of bounds for the players. They cannot move in its interior anymore.
- There can never be zones of the same size.
Zuniq’s goal is simple: to be the last player able to move. The player who cannot make a move (either because there are none possible or because it would make a zone with repeated size) is the loser. Draws are impossible.
Simple race game with the objective of being first to win the Rally.The pieces are nice, small cars (the Buggies in the title).
Educational game for children. They fly across Europe and learn a little geography. The winner is the first player who has visited the airports and also answer a question about each city.

There are two ways to win ALL OUT - EITHER be the first to get rid of all your cards, OR be the first to collect a set of ten cards with matching icons.

Players collect sets of 3-10 character cards with the same icon. In his turn player plays set in front of him face up or add card with a matching icon to any set already on the table. 
Player may also play an event card.

Player who gets rid of all your cards or has a set of 10 matching cards on the table in front of him must shout "ALL OUT!!!". He is the winner.

This is an educational game to help with speech and communication skills using a rain forest theme.

A Game of Syntax, Morphology, and Phonology.

Contents include:
- Rain forest gameboard
- 5 card decks
- 21 token cards
- 7 token boards
- 4 playing pieces

There are 2 versions included in the rules:
- 1: secure more token cards than any other player.  You receive a token card when you land on the "Explore the rain forest" square and answer a question correctly.
- 2: be the first to secure the three specific tokens that correspond to the token board held by the player.  You do this by landing on a desired space that matches a space on your token board and correctly answer a question.

Ja, See? Express is a free Print & Play game that is inspired by older, more traditional dice-rolling games, but distills the rules down into only the most critical and exciting elements. This allows for a quicker and easier game, while still retaining all of the edge-of-the-seat dice-rolling thrills that players love.

In turn, players roll five dice and may choose to keep their results, or re-roll any number of dice up to two additional times, while trying to make the highest point-scoring combination of die results. At the end of their turn, players record their score on the custom score pad, and play proceeds to the next player. Play continues in this manner until all players have filled their score sheet, or until one player has scored 50 points. The first player to 50 points is the winner.

This version of the ruleset also includes bonus rules for Ja, See? Express Express, the fastest and most exciting way to play Ja, See? Express yet devised.

A PDF file containing the rules and components is now available in the Files section.

Hank Powerman’s dream was to run the perfect golf resort.  He’d just won the one million dollar lottery; problem is, he doesn’t know how to design one.  That’s where you come in.  Hank will hire you to design the world’s greatest golf course.

In this unique card game, players must build a golf course by buying holes, and amenities (IE Pro Shop, Pool area, etc.), then using your "Fore!" cards that you were dealt with in the beginning of the game, by constructing your course, laying down the cards on the table making sure the cart path connects with each other.  Continue to lay the cards until you have all 18 holes on your course, but beware your opponents can sabotage your course with gophers and floods.  Don't forget, you also need employees to help you run Hank's Resort to help you combat your opponent's disasters.

"Its a game that's Par for the Course!"

User summary:
Advance The Colors is the first widely-published set of 1:1 ratio rules to cover the American Civil War. The rules focus on simulating tactical and skirmish-level engagements between company or regimental-sized units with an ideal size limit of between 200-300 figures per side. Each miniature represents a single soldier or artillery piece and the rules provide for officer ranks, command and control, and campaign development of individual soldiers through heroic actions. The rules we're written primarly for 15mm miniatures but can easily be used with 5mm-25mm.

boardgame: A Giant Game Sheet of Star Wars
description: An 87cm x 61cm board,featuring various routes across the galaxy, from The Rebel Alliance start area and ending at theImperial Starship Battle Station Death Star. Players roll dice to advance along one of the routes, taking detours where indicated. The first player to reach the Death Star wins the game. The publisher claims this is an official adaption of the Twentieth Century Fox Film.

This is a handheld action/skill game. By tilting and rotating the tube, try to maneuver the ball around the inside track. It's tricky but FUN for the whole family! The perfect score is 23, achieved by moving the ball back and forth across the track 4 times.

Components:

     Roll Around game unit, 
     two steel balls

Excerpted from the back of the book

Best of Nexus
The best of the classic NEXUS files, completely revised and updated for the Captain's Edition!

Featured Scenarios & Items: Complete rules for the ISC (including Order of Battle, Counters, and rules) published here for the first time! Federation & Empire tactic notes & combat missions. Scenarios: The Hydran Expedition and The Second Federation-Kzinti War.

Also includes: Klingon Battlecruisers (four previously unpublished SSDs); Tactics; and Academy (the Commander and his cadets discuss classic SFB strategy & maneuvers).

User Summary

Too Close to the Flame

Featured Scenarios: Kzintis battle Lyrans, but they are all 'Too Close to the Flame. Klingons trap a Federation Battlecruiser with Betrayal at Oxvind V. The Masters force captive starships to play the cruel game of PhaserTag. The ISC tries to stop a war, but Accursed are the Peacemakers. Face a deadly solitaire challenge in Spinner's Moon. Play in a 500-point Squadron Tournament!

Also Included: Various extras & updates for Federation & Empire; SFB Universe Timeline; Tactics; and Four New Tournament Ships w/ SSDs.

Excerpted from the back of the book

A Sense of Obligation

Featured Scenarios: Stop the Jindarians! Rescue hostages from Tholian space. Intercept a convoy! There's a Q-ship inside your convoy. Take a Federation Mauler into a Nest of Andromedan Vipers! Hunt down the Klingon mauler Siegebreaker. Tackle a new monster: the Mulakee. Rescue a Convoy from a zone where it's Hotter than Blazes. X-ships meet in a Slugfest.

Also included: The Frax (16 pages of new SSDs); Eight New Ships (8 additional SSDs); Index (of all elements in the SFB Universe); More Federation & Empire info; and more

The Joy of Conversation Game

Turn over a “Topic Card” and then everyone has 45 seconds to write down whatever they can think of about in relation to the topic; topics range from New York City to Jay Leno to Google to Las Vegas.  When time is up, players move their coffee beans up their respective containers (boards) based on the number of original ideas they had.  To change a topic:  “Cream it” and move on.  To have the other players to accept your offbeat answers:  Sweeten them up with a “Sugar Packet”... and you score.

Contents:  300 topics, 10 Instant Coffee Cards, 12 Sugar Packets, 12 Creamer Packets, 6 Coffee Beans, 6 Individual  Coffee Cup Scoring Boards, 6 Score Pads and a 45 second Sand Timer.

The game board shows the economic resources of eight iconic kiwi organizations. Each player is in a race to own these resources. To win, a player needs to own all the resources (natural, human and capital) of two organizations. The first player to do this is the winner.

Players gain resources by either landing on an unowned resource and buying it from the bank or by trading with other players. The enterprising nature of Gumption is encouraged by deal-breaking squares on the game board and by risk cards. The playing pieces are familiar New Zealand icons which players use to move around the game board.

The game comes with a booklet that highlights each of the organizations depicted on the board.

Each denomination of the money used in Gumption profiles an invention that has started a New Zealand business.

There are three different kinds of cards in the game:

Risk Cards
Players take a Risk card and follow its instructions. Many Risk Cards can be traded with other players.

Resource Cards
Every resource shown on the Game Board has an associated resource card. This card shows the income that is payable when another player lands on the resource. It also shows the income payable if all three resources are owned and if the player has Future Technology. The resource cards can be traded with anyone except the bank.

Future Technology Cards
Players can invest in research and development and buy Future Technology, which will increase their income. This is done once players own all the resources of one organisation. There are Risk Cards that allow players to buy Future Technology without first owning all three resources.
publishers: HOD Business at Ormiston Senior College

description:
The Slippery Slope is a board game designed to help players open up and deal with their emotions and feelings that are associated with the grief they feel when separated, going through a divorce or living through the aftermath of a single parent family.

Each player selects either a frog, race car or person, and starts moving down the slope.  The slope is labeled for the stages you go through – Denial, Bargaining, Depression, Forgiveness, and Acceptance.

Each player receives one Grace Card at the beginning of the game.  As each player rolls the die they will move 1, 2,or 3 spaces.  As they move ahead they will have to either select an Action Card (a question designed to ask – What would you do…) or a Feelings Card (a question designed to ask – How would you feel…)  Every now and then they may land on a miss a turn or move ahead or behind space.  Depending on their roll, they may also acquire additional Grace Cards.  The Grace Cards can be used at any time if they select a question they may choose to pass on.  First one to reach Acceptance wins the game.

A "roll a die and move" round a track game where one buys/charges royalties on patents rather than streets in the Monopoly manner when players land on these squares. Having adjacent sets of patents enables enhanced royalties to be charged.
 There is an added quiz element whereby when a play lands on an un-owned square, they may obtain the property for free if they can answer a question of the "Who invented ...." form.

FUTAB is definitely a chess game. A game that will make you feel on the court, with all occurrences of a real game. You can experience the thrill of putting a scoring pass, connecting a center head, rally the contrary in their own area or win with a goal on the hour. I can define a tactical game and beat your rival.

FUTAB is a simulator of a soccer match. The objective is to defeat the opposing player throws the ball into the arch rival advancing through a series of boxes. To play you only need two dice and a "ball" (tab, coin, button, etc).

The board has represented the movements and plays more common in football and, through the roll of the dice and a good choice for best play, you can move forward on the grid court to reach the arch rival. The game is played to 90 dice throws.

FUTAB es definitivamente el ajedrez del fútbol. Un juego que te hará sentir dentro de la cancha, con todas las incidencias de un auténtico partido. Viví la adrenalina de meter un pase gol, conectar un centro de cabeza, pelotear al contrario en su propia área ó ganar con un gol sobre la hora. Definí una táctica de juego y vencé a tu rival.

FUTAB es un simulador de un partido de fútbol. El objetivo del mismo es vencer al jugador contrario introduciendo la pelota en el arco rival avanzando a través de una serie de casilleros. Para jugarlo solo se necesitan dos dados y una "pelota" (ficha, botón, etc).

El tablero tiene representados los movimientos y las jugadas más comunes en los partidos de fútbol y, a través del lanzamiento del dado y una buena elección de la jugada más conveniente, se puede ir avanzando en la cancha cuadriculada hasta alcanzar el arco rival. La partida se juega a 90 lanzamientos de dado.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Two classic games - Hot Potato and Tag - are rolled into one with the talking musical disc!

Yrrr' It! is the fun new game that will have the whole neighborhood hopping, skipping and rolling around with excitement! Music starts to begin "Hot Potato" as the disc is passed from player to player. If you are the last player to touch the disc before the buzzer then... Yrrr' It! To avoid getting tagged, all other players must jump, hop, skip, and even somersault away as commands are spoken from the talking disc. Careful, you'll never know what the talking disc will say or how much time you have before the buzzer sounds in this wildly hilarious game where the winner is truly up in the air!
Players try to slap the puck into the opponent's goal with his stick in the hockey-board.

Cadaco Match Point Tennis takes the Cadaco Bas-Ket game and adds a twist - rackets.  The two players volley back and forth over the net playing by the rules of tennis using the standard flipper mechanism.  Each player also has a racket which he can use to block shots.

Very unusual board game from the late 70's. Each player is a mountaineer and must climb the Mont Blanc to win the game. However, the player has to earn experience points to get the skills to climb mountains, so there are 22 mountains to conquer. Each climb is harder than the previous one. You don'Ät have to climb them all, you can choose which to climb. You might, for example, climb Number One (Aiguille du Belvédère) successfully, and then try Number Four (Aiguille Purtscheller), and so on.

Each climb is illustrated for the player, with various points along the route when equipment (Alea) cards might be asked for, the more equipment you have, the better for you. And the Descent (Rappel) might cause you problems.

The board is very attractive, showing the mountain ranges. There is a curious box in the right hand corner, on which you place two tiny green frogs. These frogs move during the game and when they reach certain points on the track, the weather changes (Meteo). This weather change can be catastrophic. You might not to be able to get down a mountain you have climbed and a helicopter might be needed to get you off the mountain.

The game looks great with all the Climbs being illustrated in some detail. The playing pieces look good but are completely impractible. They are plastic Picks, in 6 colours. And they fall over far too easily. And not to forget, the two green frogs.

In my game there was a card asking me to buy their next game, which would be about skiing. However, I got no reply and I never did see a second game.

Players must travel around the Austrian city of Vienna in a tram, All the points of arrival has facts about the stops along the route. A die is used for movement. Quiz questions need answering at some point and the correct answers are within the rules. The winner is the first player to complete his route and return home.

I only have a reproduction of this game and I have no idea when it was first made. My edition is from the 1980's but the original must be over a hundred years old.

Nénufable is a wooden game for children (and adults).

It's a race between frogs where the goal is to be the first frog under the balcony of the princess to be kissed by her.

Players can move ahead, put a stone or waterlily to make a road, push opponent in the water or move the princess to another balcony.

But beware... there is a sorceress in the castle who randomnly move from balcony to balcony, and if you finish under her balcony she push you in the water, and you have to restart from the begining...

Bistouris is a game of plastic surgery.
Each player is a patient AND a doctor.

The game takes place in two rounds.

First round, each player roll dice, move on the board and draw cards to create their own (and awful) bodies.
When the body of each player is done, the second round begin.

At the begining of the second round, each player draw a "perfect body" card to know what they need to change on their bodies.
Now players roll dice and move again on the board and try to reach on "surgery" squares to have surgery from other players... but beware... surgical procedures may fail and you end up with a body worse than before and you have twice as much work !!!
Other players bet on the result of the surgery and if it fail, the surgeon must explain what's went wrong.

The first player who manages to have a perfect body (as his "perfect body" card") is the winner

Tabakitaba is an educational and fun game on tabacco.
A bit of monopoly, a hint of trivial pursuit, an inch of strategy game and some educational datas on tabacco.

One player play the tabacco industry and other players are citizens.

The goal for the tabacco industry is to convert up to citizens to smoking.
The goal for the citizens is to safeguard their health and stop smoking.

Players move on a board (the city) consting of several kind of squares (events, temptation, disease risks, tobaccological consultation) and a hex map (the rural) where citizens and tobacco industry can plant cereals or tobacco.

At the end, the player with the best health is the winner and the tobacco industry too if there are more smokers than non-smokers.

"The waltz of the dough ! The quest for loot!"

Western is a card game between Wanted and Cash n' Guns.

The goal is to be the first player to earn an amount of dough depending of the number of players (400.000 to 500.000 dough).

Each player begin the game with an amount of dough and some cards in hand.
There is also a bank with some money (or a money train). 
Players use cards and a round die to attack each other, trying to stole some money (or lose some if the fail !), they can also use "dirty tricks" or other protection cards and sometimes fight for the money train.
The game ends when a player has 400.000 dough.

There is 108 cards in more than 20 kinds.

Rules in french can be found on the editor website (english and other languages are coming).

boardgame: Battle of 4 Armies: Restoring the Crown of Chip
description:
From designer Nathan Hansen comes a quick paced, deep decision rich, abstract strategy BattlessonTM game, Battle of 4 Armies. Match your skills against up to three opponents, for your goal is the Crown of Chip! Through the judicious use of unit placement and maneuver, you struggle to capture opposing units to knock them out of play, or hold the majority of named spaces on the board. With pressure for the center of the map and maneuver around its edges, players have myriad ways to approach every turn of this absorbing game. Make your move in the Battle of 4 Armies!

Game Data:

Complexity: 1.5 on a 9 scale
Solitaire Suitability: 2 on a 9 scale
Scale: Each unit represents a single troop of fantasy warriors.

Game Components:

•    One 8.5” x 11” color game map
•    One 2-page, color instructions sheet
•    One BattlessonTM sheet
•    20 color, die-cut 1” mounted game pieces

Read My Lips! version for younger people where they have 20 seconds to "lip" words and sayings for everyone else to guess. Words are from 6 categories: Person, Place, TV/movie, Thing, Music, Saying. Sometimes easy, sometimes not so easy.

Be the first player to correctly guess two words/sayings in all six categories.

Publisher Blurb:

There may be no one in the entire world whose skill with the bow rivals that of Daniella Meris. Not only is her aim unequaled, but her mastery of the Ki Domains and her control of energy allow her to face entire battalions alone.

Warrior - Light Empire - Level 45

Innate Abilities - Ranged Attack (16 inches), Advanced Deployment

Ripple in Water (Ki, Ranged Attack) - Distance (16 inches), Roll two dice and keep the highest result.

Chasing the Moon (Ki, Ranged Attack) - Distance (20 inches), If this attack causes damage, the target unit suffers Seal Lv2, Effect 15.

Shooting Star (Ki, Ranged Attack) - Distance (16 inches), +1 Attack. When this attack hits the target, all units within 2 inches of the target character also suffer the Attack

Divine Flames (Ki, Ranged Attack) - Distance (16 inches), +3 Attack. When this attack hits the target, all units within 3 inches of the target character also suffer the attack.

This pewter figure is unpainted and unassembled. Requires additional Anima Tactics figures to play.

Publisher Blurb:

Master strategist and unrivaled warrior, at twenty-one years of age Yuri Olson is the youngest person in history to achieve the rank of Imperial General. Considered by all the right hand of the Warlord Elías Van Horsman by his own merits, Yuri is also the secret leader of field operations of the Hand of the Empress, the special corps of agents of Elisabetta Barbados.

Leader Warrior - Light Empire - Level 55

Innate Abilities - Critical Mastery, Initiative.

Unbreakable Will - If Yuri suffers Damage, he gains +1 to his Attack during the rest of the game.

King's Tower (Ki, Effect, Reactive) - Yuri or a friendly unit within his Control Zone gains +3 Defense against a specific Attack.

Queen's Round (Ki, Attack) - Roll two dice and keep the highest result. If the attack causes a Critical Hit, deal +5 to Damage.

Tactical Mastery (Special, Effect, Reactive) - This ability may be used at the end of the Recovery Phase. All friendly units with Line of Sight to Yuri recover an additional Action Point.

Limit: Once per game.

War Cry (Special, Effect) - When War Cry is used, friendly units within Yuri's Control Zone receive +1 Attack and +1 Damage until the end of the Turn and lose any negative state counters. The bonus is kept even if the units leave Yuri's Control Zone.

Limit: Once per game.

This pewter figure is unpainted and unassembled. Requires additional Anima Tactics figures to play.

Each player picks a game piece and place it on its image on the board. High roll of dice determines who goes first.
Set up Freddie, decide whether to play for real cash or play stuff, pace your ante. Turn on TV football game - It's party time!

Object of the Game: You are betting on a football game so if you win the money, ah...you win! Game play is determined by the TV game. Make first downs and win the Freddie Pot in the middle of the field. Make it to the end or be the farthest along the board or game's end to win the big bucks in the Pom Pot in the end zone! Game Contents: 8 playing pieces and stands, Freddie Football and stand, pair of dice, play money, game board and rules sheet

Components:

     1 game board.
     8 playing pieces and stands.
     Freddie Football with stand.
     Pair of dice.
     Play money (80 1s, 5s and 10s).
     Rules sheet.

Publisher Blurb:

The 3D Castles Expansion Set provides you with additional modular card stock castle pieces to allow you to customize the shape and size of your Grunt castle. It also provides an additional 3D city and siege towers, ballisti, and more.

Jarmial is a fantasy card game close to korsar in the mechanic.

Players fights for realms of 5 colors trying to collect a set of 4 realms of the same color or 5 realms of the 5 colors

Players use creatures of different strengths and colors and spells to win the realms.
When someone plays a color on a realm, other players need to use another color to fight on it.
When a player collects a set of 4 realms of a kind or 5 realms of the 5 colors, the round ends, then players earn points depending of what they have collected and they start anorther round.

After some rounds (determined at the beginning of the game) the winner is the player with most points.

Sesquipedalian is a word game, intended primarily as an vocab-building game for kids. It shares mechanics with party games like Cranium and Balderdash. Players move through the board by rolling a die, and complete color coded actions corresponding to their space on the board. Actions include:

- illustrating a word
- acting out a word
- guessing the definition of a word
- making up definitions of a word for other players to guess
- guessing the correct category of a word (such as "astonishing actions")

The game comes with a small dictionary, with a focus on rare, multisyllabic and/or unusual-sounding words. Also included are rules for other simple paper-and-pencil games that can be played using the dictionary, including Hangman, Ghost, and Word Squares.

18NAC is an 18xx game that includes railroads across the North American Continent. 18NAC is played on a 55" by 34" map depicting northern Mexico, southern Canada, and the continental United States. The game includes 29 U.S. railroads, 14 in the East and 15 in the West, as well as 5 Mexican companies and the Canadian Pacific. The game includes several mechanics that are unique to the 18xx genre, including a system of subsidiaries (rather than privates) that allow players to customize their railroads, an anonymous auction, and land grant companies.

Publisher Blurb:

History of War (HoW) presents the Second World War from the tactical point of view of units. Retaliatory weapons, atomic bombs or personal names like those of generals are deliberately excluded from the game.

The goal of the game is a conquest of land cards until 15 win points are accumulated. This is accomplished by bringing together units from all three arenas of the war: land, sea, and air.

The Axis starter deck contains 55 cards and a rulesheet. The deck is fully playable. Each player needs their own deck to play.

In Bridal Shower Pictionary game each team chooses a Picturist for the first round.  A Picturist is the person who will sketch for their team.  One Pictionary card is selected out of the bowl.  Each Picturist looks at the bridal shower theme word and begin sketching for their team when time is started.  Remember not to use letters of numbers in your sketch.  The first team to guess the word correctly in one minute or less scores a point on the score card.  The Picturist rotates for each team and play continues until one team has scored 10 points.

Game for 2 teams

With three different ways to play, the Teletubbies Match & Stamp game is both an activity kit and a game. It comes with 4 cans of different-colored Play-Doh; 12 Teletubbies stampers that fit nicely into their own sturdy holder; 6 Teletubbies cards, each with 4 different images; and 4 plastic trays, plus a wooden roller for rolling out the Play-Doh. Players match the pictures on the stampers to the pictures on their Teletubbies card. The corresponding Teletubbies images are then pressed into the Play-Doh with the stampers.

Just roll the dough, pick a stamper and make a picture.
In the beginners' game, the youngest fans will search for "lookalikes," matching stamper pictures with pictures on their cards.  In the next two games, the stamper pictures are hidden:  Pick a stamper - who's got matching picture?

Components:

     12 Teletubbies Stampers
     4 Cans of Playdoh
     6 Teletubbies Cards
     4 Plastic Trays
     Stamper Holder
     Wooden Roller

Rosetta is a boardless tile-laying game with hex-tiles. Each tile (except 3 extra-tiles showing a rose) shows one of the numbers from 1 to 7. They are available in four player colours and 7 times as white. The white ones are the general supply, forming three stacks, so the three top tiles are available. Each player has the tiles of his own colour as a "personal supply".
Gameplay: On a player`s turn, he can choose either a white top tile or a tile from his personal supply and place it adjacent to any tile(s) already placed on the table. It is allowed to place any white tile adjacent to any other tile, but coloured tiles must not be adjacent to other coloured ones. Aim of the game is to create as many "perfect Rosettas" as possible: groups of seven tiles, with a tile of your colour as the centre and 6 white tiles with the other six numbers surrounding it, which is 10 points worth in the final score. If one or more tiles of 7 tiles show the same number, the "doubles" are subtracted from the 10 points. Rose-tiles are "jokers" and stand for any number a scoring player wants.
The game ends, when all tiles are placed, or all coloured tiles are surrounded.

boardgame: Operation C: Tödliche Kämpfe in einer vergessenen Zeit
description:
Operation C is an german Dungeon crawler Prototyp!

Description:
4 heroes, a secret research station, forbidden experiments and a day where everything goes wrong. Take control over an arsenal of 25 weapons. Play 30 exciting missions. Did I say that in the research station dinosaurs were bred as bioweapons? This will be a hell of a day. Welcome to the CHIMAERE.

Operation C is an adventure board game for 4 players. The players represent 4 different heroes, which have to complete various missions in the CHIMAERA research station. Equipped with a multitude of abilities, equipment and weapons, the heroes have to face powerful bioweapons, created in the facility using dinosaur DNA. The goal of each mission is to find the Objective sector of the mission. Each Round the players explore the facility, consisting of multiple sectors (dinosaur cages, laboratories, open space...). The sectors have the form of floor tiles, which are arranged side by side in a specific and different layout for each mission. When the players discover the Objective sector, they need to perform a specific task in it.

Players take on the role of Jesus’ Disciples, traveling with Jesus through Palestine and performing deeds consistent with His teachings.  But the actions the players will take will not always be received favorably by the three factions that will determine the fate of Judea — the Jews, who alternate between loving and resenting Jesus; Rome, which is not interested in Jesus but simply wants to impose civility and order, and the Pharisees, who abhor Jesus and who, as their anger increases, are looking for a member of Jesus’ inner circle to betray Him…

Each turn, players receive 4 actions, which they may spend traveling to one of the cities in Palestine, drawing action cards, spending action cards to perform a Deed in a city, or requesting Jesus to perform a Deed.  Deeds come in four categories (preach, heal, exorcise a demon, or show compassion), and the cost of performing a Deed increases with each Deed performed in that category.  But players can call on Jesus to perform a Deed, which resets that category's cost track and awards players present in the area with Gospel tokens.  Most of the Deeds also result in consequences on one or more of the political tracks, representing the crowds, the Pharisees, or Rome.

The game ends either when the Rome track reaches its end (indicating that Jesus and the Disciples have attracted too much attention and Rome decides to intervene), or when the player who was dealt the Judas card elects to end the game, and receives the payout indicated on the Pharisees track.

The players' score is comprised of victory points received from the Deeds they performed, the Gospel tokens they accumulated, and the conditions described on their secret Goal cards that they received at the start of the game.

Similar to Triominos but the numbers (pips) are on the sides of the triangular tiles rather than the corners.

45 tiles with a number from 1 to 4 on each side.

Game play is similar - play a tile on your turn but there are shape templates in the instruction booklet that have to be followed.  Try to use all your tiles as points are deducted per pip left at the end

Instructions in English, but game is language independent.

First issue of Recon...by Fire!

From the table of contents:

Luckiest Guy in the Marines by Eddie Zeman
A Brief History of VASL by Rodney Kinney
ASL in Desert Storm by Eddie Zeman
Hard Point Tarawa by Heat of Battle
ASL Matinee: Red Barricades CG IV by Heat of Battle
Clubbing 101 by Matt Shostak
Fear and Loathing in Baltimore by Rob Seulowitz
Crossfire: Blazing Chariots by Rob Banozic and Rich Summers
Crossing Open Ground by Matt Romey
Evolution of Marine Firepower by Eddie Zeman and Steven Swann

Scenarios:

RBF I #1 Weather the Sturm - A German Company reinforced with tank destroyers and artillery are dug in and must hold off an elite Russian attack force during the opening days of Operation Bagration (1944).

RBF I #2 Aggravation at Agrigento - A dug in Italian company with artillery support has to hold off an American infantry company as General Patton’s army rolls through Sicily (1943).

RBF I #3 South Park - A German infantry company supported by assault guns attempts to silence and artillery battery. The Americans throw together a scratch force of infantry and armor to repel them during the Battle of the Bulge (1944).

RBF I #4 Used and Abused - Japanese armor and infantry attempt to recapture an airfield on the island of Biak. The airfield is held by an American infantry company (1944).

RBF I #5 Paper Line - The “Paper Line” is a defense line in Belgium that the French envisioned using to stop the Germans. In this engagement French armored cars with infantry support arrive on the scene just as scout elements of a German panzer division arrive. The French must keep the Germans from securing vital a vital road junction (1940).

RBF I #6 Desert Fortress - As the Germans created a foothold in Tobruk (1941), the Australians were sent in to dig them out.

RBF I #7 Into the Cauldron - In the first push on the factories of Stalingrad, hastily assembled factory workers and stragglers attempt to stop an scratch group of Germans with engineers and an assault gun to support them in their attempt to take a factory (August, 1942).

RBF I #8 Germeter by Meter - Dug in and fortified, German infantry have to hold off an American company supported by tank destroyers during the battle for the Hurtgen Forest (1944).

There's a way to upgrade anything. Want more acceleration? Add a 'charger or install a short-throw shifter. Need better handling? Let's tweak the suspension and/or the brakes. The upgrades aren't just limited to vehicle performace, either. Need to eek out a litle more range out of your primary weapon? How about some armor-piercing rounds? The South Tejas Speed Shop has the toys you need to give you the ultimate performance edge ... including a few that you might not have expected - don't discount the importance of fuzzy dice until you've tried 'em(!)

Tuner Cards allow players to equip their Outrider vehicles with performance enhancing equipment and upgrades, allowing them more options to customize and balance their vehicles. The cards also add a slight RPG element to the game, as many cards add 'character' to the drivers/vehicles
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the box:

A fun game where children learn shapes and colors. Spin to collect game tiles to complete their colorful mystery picture.

No reading required.

Deduction game.
Components: 54 wooden tiles featuring faces with a variety of eyes, noses and smiles, 18 control cards showing 18 face variations, red and white plastic counters.
Game for 2 players, one the informant and one the detective. 54 tiles are placed face up in the lid of the box or on the table. The informant selects one card from the 18 control cards and copies the face onto a piece of paper secretly The card is returned to the pack and the paper hidden from the detective. One of the colours, red, yellow or white is also written on the paper.
The detective then attempts to deduce the features of the face by presenting a tile. The informant places a red infomation counter  for each correct feature; eyes, nose, mouth, colour. This continues until the detective knows the selected identity. Twice per round the informant can refuse to give any information and indicates this by placing a white counter. At the end of the round players reverse roles. The winner is the player who, as detective, collected the fewest information counters.

In a world of Nomadic Hamlets, Temporary Towns and Moving Castles, you are the Captain King of a Rolling Rampart who has come up against an enemy Fortress in a battle of siege engines and fortifications.
 Who will siege the day?

In Rolling Ramparts, players decide whether they will attack, defend or deploy the white flag for each of the six sections of their moving castle. Each choice has both benefits and drawbacks. Battle Dice are rolled and combined with their section's strength and damage is applied. Captain Kings continue the siege until one of the castles has three sections reduced to zero strength.

Rolling Ramparts was designed specifically to be included in the "36-Dice" family introduced by Asmadi Games.

This game came in two formats one that used a die but earlier versions came with a spinner for randomization. The players are printed on the board and movement is the puck from square to square. There are printed squares on the board that if you take the puck to will can cause penalties to the opposing player in various ways including giving you a penalty shot on goal from a designated spot on the board that is also resolved with a die roll.

Issue game of Tactics #36 in Japan.
Designed by Ryuji Yamashita.
An operational wargame covering the battle between German and Soviet from January to April of 1942 around Demjansk.
The game engine is regular move + attack with exploitation.
The map is covered with dense woods.  The supply rule is severer than usual wargame due to its climate.

An operational wargame covering the German last offensive in Eastern front March, 1945.
This offensive operation is done around Balaton lake in Hungary.
The game uses move + attack + exploitation engine.
Victory is decided by VP.  The most important VP locations are Dunapentele and Dunafolvar along Duna river.
This game is Akira Ishikawa's final work just before his death.

User summary:
A straightforward, large scale skirmish game for the Zulu and other colonial wars. These rules are 1:1 ratio with each figure representing an individual soldier and are intended for 25/28mm scale miniatures although other scales can be used. Each type figure has a unique set of four abilities that are used during movement and combat (Close Combat Skill, Agility/Horsemanship/Firing Skill, Initiative). Special figures such as officers have unique abilities beyond normal infantry. The rules are simple and fast-playing with a strong emphasis on historical flavor and playability.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Nines is a simple game that uses two decks of standard cards with jokers included, and is based almost entirely on luck.

Players are dealt nine cards each, and they proceed to line them up into three vertical columns (3 cards per column). They each then turn over two cards that are in different columns.

The goal of Nines is to have the fewest points at game end by getting columns with matching cards (e.g. three 4's in one column). Matching cards in a column essentially "cancels out" that column and the total value of those three cards is zero.

After all cards are dealt, a single card is flipped over from the top of the draw pile. Starting to the left of dealer, a player's turn consists of drawing a single card either from the discard pile or the draw deck. If the player takes a card from the draw deck, they may look at it and either discard it or replace one of the cards in their current play area (can replace either face up or face down). If they choose to replace a card, they have to discard the one they replaced. Taking a card from the discard pile means they have to replace one of their cards, and then discard the one they replaced.

The game end is triggered when one person has all of their cards turned face up. Then, every other person gets one more turn. On this final turn, players do get the option of turning one of their own cards over.

After the game is over, the players turn over any face down cards. If a face down card matches the face up cards in its column, that column of cards counts as zero points. All points are then tallied.

The cards all have different values. Jokers are worth negative three points, aces and kings are both worth zero points, jacks and queens are ten points each, and the numbered cards have point values equal to the number on the card.

This game is concerned with the acquiring of Assets. The Assets are acquired by making bids using cards. Every player has 56 Bid Cards, that range from 1 to 50 + 6 blank cards. When a player bids for an Asset, he selects one card from his holding. All bids are simultaneous. You don't have to bid if you do not want to. Bids are revealed and the highest acquires the Asset and all Bid Cards used go out of the game. Selling Assets is done in the same way but now the owner definitely won't bid but will collect the winning bid in cash. Again, all Bids made go out of the game.

Movement across the board requires no dice, you just move one Asset space to another each turn. You must follow the 'flight paths' of the planes printed on the board.

The objective is to collect Assets in the same group (Example: Group: Energy; Assets needed Nuclear, Gas and Coal). When you have a set of three Assets, you may declare it and claim a Blue Chip for that set. A Blue Chip holding doubles the value of a set. When 10 Blue Chips have been declared, the game ends. Players announce the value of their Assets, add in their cash, and thge highest total wins.

boardgame: Community Challenge for Miami: Fort Lauderdale
description:
This is a Florida trivia game about the Miami and Fort Lauderdale area. The board features a variety of local landmarks and tourist attractions.

At the beginning of the game each player is given four "challenge cards" which correspond to spaces on the board. The object of the game is to become the first player who "captures" and controls all four of his or her challenge spaces. Players capture a space by correctly answering a trivia question. Spaces can be taken away from another player by demanding a challenge when a contestant's token lands in the square.

Includes two booklets -- Level I and Level II, with Level II questions being more difficult. Also includes the board, six tokens, a die, 24 challenge cards, and a storage insert. 
.

Manufacturer's Description:
Word Ace is the electronic word racing game seen by millions- a final four finisher on the American Inventor television show! Word Ace gives players a letter and number (A, 9) and they have to be the first to find a word that meets certain criteria, such as "a word that starts with 'A' and has 9 letters" (Armadillo!). Multiple variations keep the game fresh, while teaching vocabulary skills at the same time.

Components:

     Word Ace electronic console, 
     4 wipe-off boards, 
     4 dry erase markers
     Requires 3AAA batteries.


While the game finished second during Season 1 of American Inventor, said competition was held in 2006 and Hasbro announced during the Season Finale that they would "work to make it a reality".

boardgame: Die Siedler von Catan: Das Kartenspiel – Sonderkarte 2007 – Arnd, der findige Knappe
description:
This is a special card for the Siedler von Catan card game distributed in special fairs usually by mr. Klaus Teuber itself.

boardgame: Flipping Out: The House Remodel Game
description:
Based on the theory of restoring houses quickly, and for profit, the game of flipping out combines worker placement mechanics with the unpredictable nature of the business of flipping houses.

The game was conceived in 2007, and it will debut in the 2011 Solitaire Game Design contest.

A player will have effort which they may expand over the course of a calender month. The core game will have:

Available on BGG in June 2011:
2 house boards
2 player boards
5 project decks 
1 buyer deck
1 rule book

To be provided by the player:
1 pawn
12 "effort" markers (cubes)
6 additional markers (for skills and value)
1 monthly calender.

Goal:

The goal of the game will be to successfully sell the house by the end of the month.

Playing the Game:

The general course of play will be to assign effort blocks, resolve effects of placement, and advance a calender day. At the end of the month (or when 4 projects are complete), the player will consult they buyer deck to see if they can successfully sell the house.

Many cards will provide "curve-balls" that will usually be up to the player to resolve with careful planning. Rich in theme, a player will note that every cause and effect represents actual events experienced by the designer in his career as a home builder (and owner).

Them! is a solo horror board game where a player will be on the run from a horrible creature, only hoping to survive until sunrise. With 20 minutes left to survive a player will need to plan an efficient escape route through the forest.

Drawing a card representing each minute, a player will follow the instructions and draw his or her path along the board. After all his "time" is up, it will be up to him or her to make sure he or she was not caught by the creature. A separate creature deck will contain the activities of the creature, and if lines are crossed, the creature wins!

This game will use a unique color change action requiring the user to change the color of his or her marker, along with that of the creature (at certain intervals). This will be the indicator that the creature was close enough to find the player, or not.

The game will consist of:

A 24 card player deck
A 24 card creature deck.
A set of template forest terrains.

The player will provide 5 colored crayons or pencils as well as three tokens to complete the game.

A multiplayer game covering the Bakumatsu era, from Blackship to Boshin war.
http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bakumatsu
"High purpose men" were anti-shogunate organisations
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shishi_(organization)

Playable from 3 to 6 players. Each player controls important persons of the era.
The game is played during 4 preparing war turns and a final Boshin war turn.
Victory is decided by collecting VPs during play. VPs are given according to status track and outcome of the Boshin war.

Second edition:
1 rule book (color)
1 map (A3 size double-sided printing)
1 counter sheet (15mm square)
6 sheets of cards (54 cards)
3 dice (6-sided dice)Number of players: 3-6
Play time: about 90-120 minutes

From the back of the box:
Contents: 4 boards with perforations, 126 banknotes, 10 fixed cards, 4 insurance policies, 80 random cards, 80 pegs, 1 die.
The purpose of the game is to trade goods in such a way that you can keep your business going, avoid bankruptcy, if possible make a profit and become the foremost firm on the market. Each player has a business, in other words a board with columns for 6 kinds of goods, and a starting capital of $100,000. Each player buys one kind of goods. There are 6 different kinds of goods with various prices. Before a player can buy one kind of goods he must throw a certain number on the dice. When he does so, he can go ahead and buy a maximum of three units of the chosen kind of goods. If the player throws a smaller number, he must then take a card from the pack of random cards and follow the instructions. These can be helpful or unhelpful. Once a player has several kinds of goods he can start selling and by doing so try to make a profit. However, he can sell only goods of one kind each time, and only - as with buying - when he throws the right number on the dice. Once he has thrown this number the bank gives him  the specified selling price which contains a certain profit margin. If the player throws a smaller number, he may not sell and must draw a random card whose instructions he must follow.
What makes the game exciting is that all the players have the chance to bid for goods being bought or sold. In other words, when goods are being bought each player can make his own bid and acquire the goods. The same applies to selling. The specified selling price can be undercut, giving the player an opportunity to find a buyer for his goods. But remember: the purpose is still to make a profit in the end.
The winner is the player who reaches the topmost square with his goods. The other players then count up their capital (goods and money) and work out their relative positions on the market.

Playing as one of six unique Squirrels your mission is to add eight acorns to your personal stockpile in the Big Tree so you won't be hungry in the winter months.

Forage and fight other Squirrels for acorns as you avoid such dangerous things such as power lines, cars, and kids with BB guns.

Setup

The playing space consists of nine location cards in a grid. The Big Tree is always placed in the middle with the other locations being placed at random. Then each card starts with a certain number of acorns noted on the card.

Each player receives a stockpile card with scoring chip, a random squirrel card, and a pawn.

Gameplay

On your turn you can choose three actions from the following:

1.) Forage for an acorn - (Roll d6 under forage)
2.) Move one space orthogonally to a new location
3.) Attempt to steal an acorn from another squirrel at the same location as you (Roll d6+skill - Highest wins)
4.) Add an acorn to your stockpile. (Only at the Big Tree)

Whenever a player rolls a six that player draws an event card and follows the instructions on the card.

Will you be the first Squirrel to stockpile eight acorns and be ready for winter?

Nothing exciting ever happened in Hicksville, a small town in the middle of nowhere – until tonight when the town has been taken over by zombies! You and a group of survivors have taken shelter in the old mansion of a coffee baron, but unfortunately a horde of zombies has followed you. Trapped in the attic, you must plan to escape before the house is overrun and you all are turned into hideous undead.

In Turned, each player takes a character card and places his character in the attic. Shuffle the nine escape plan cards, then draw a number equal to one less than the number of players; these cards are placed face up so everyone can see them. Each card shows the equipment tiles that must be collected in order to escape from the mansion room named on the card; players can work toward completing any of the escape plans. Divide the equipment tiles into three piles based on color/symbol, shuffle them, and set them down as piles from which players can draw during the game.

On a turn, a player has four action points to spend; actions consist of moving to a new room, searching for equipment, trading equipment with other players, making noise, and using equipment. When players search or make noise, they attract zombies in the mansion toward that room. If a player is in a room with a zombie, he gets infected, and the disease progresses each turn until the player becomes a zombie himself – at which point he then wants to infect other players.

The first player to escape from the mansion wins. If no one escapes – because they've all been turned into zombies – then everyone but the last player to become a zombie wins.

Just one more dance!
Every year the kindoms most powerful necromancers battle for rulership. So they send their undead minions around the deadly parcours of the central graveyard. 
The necromancer, who has sorted all his minions in the right order in his crypt, wins the crown of suprmacy and is ruler of the guild of necromancers for one year.

Remember SORRY! from 1934? We pimped the board, switched the setting to competiting necromancers and set the playing pieces to 6 each (in order of power from 1 to 6: Slime, Skeleton, Zombie, Ghoul, Vampire, Lich). A bunch of magic spell and effect cards - and "Totentanz" (Dance Macabre) was born.

All the undead have a power rank and they can't beat each others like in SORRY!, because you can only hit another undead with the same or lower power rank.

The different power ranks of the undead are giving its necromancer different points for placing (in the right order) in the crypt. For example: the slime, which is the weakest creature (Power:1) has a value of 6 points when placed on its appropriate place in the crypt, while the lich (Power: 6) gives you only 1 point!

Join the clash of bones! Just hop on the "Chariot of Death" and run over your fellow undeads, use your Zombie to attack another necromancers skeleton with the "Flail of Stone" or let your lich cast "Vivisection" across the board to hinder your opponents vampire from entering the crypt. Always a favourite: "Break in the weather - Sunshine" cast on a vampire (got your vacuum cleaner ready?)

The board consists of 9 small Noughts and Crosses areas. A player can play to any area of the nine. If the small box is won, place a large X or large O in that area. That is now out of the game as far as playing pieces on to it is concerned. The first to place three large noughts or 3 large crosses in a straight line, is the winner.

Players try to answer questions in 5 categories. The category is determined by the roll of a die. Four categories contain simple trivia questions, with the correct answer giving 1 to 3 points. If the fifth category is selected, the active player can study a picture for 30 seconds, and afterwards has to answer a question about that picture.

The goal of the game is to be the first player to reach the finish line of 43 points.

GoPlay racing cars mini magnetic travel game set. 
Usual roll and move game with spinner. Player who finishes first is the winner.

Components:  

     spinner, 
     12 counters, 
     1 tin box clip

A cycling game. 2 to 6 players. Copyright 1984 Peter English. Materials: black and white laminated board featuring a course and cartoon graphics, spinner (you can use a 6 sided die, 34 bonus tabs, 7 cutout figures for bunch and riders, 6 rider cards (Bernard, Eddy, Fausto, Jacques, Sean, Tom - functionally identical). 
Riders can be either in "the bunch" or making a break out in front or left behind. When in the bunch the rider figure remains on your card where you also place bonus tabs that are accumulated by reaching certain points in the race first and also when rolling on your turn a 4 or 5 if in the bunch, or 6. Main mechanism is roll and move along a track that includes squares for gaining bonus tabs and penalties that can move you back. The race takes the riders past fans, through an avalanche, past a protest, into a thunderstorm, up the Kremmelburg climb, across cobbles and up the Poggio climb before the finish. Each turn, each player spins the spinner (rolls the die), moves individually or stays in the bunch and and after all players the bunch moves. The bunch always moves three spaces. To break away from the bunch you must roll a 4 or more. A minimum of one rider must remain in the bunch. Generally there can only be up to 2 riders per square (1 on the Kobbleburg climb) and the bunch will swallow you back up if it catches you or leave you off the back if it passes you. Players use their bonus tabs to increase their movement rate or to increase the speed of the bunch when the bunch moves.
The strategy revolves around collecting bonus tabs and deciding when and at which part of the course to make an individual break. The winner is the first rider past the finishing post.
